content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive migration analysis report.

## Item Master Service — Supabase GCP-to-Hosted Migration Audit

---

### 1. Purpose & Responsibility

**designflow-item-master** is a dedicated Node.js service (port 5003, Cloud Run private) that manages the PLM item and design master data workflows. It is not part of the informal "five repo" description but is wired into the frontend through the BFF at `/api/item_master/*`.

**Primary domains owned:**
- **Item Library** (`/lib/*`): item creation, retrieval, item type management, item number generation with collision checking, grid layout persistence
- **Item Detail** (`/detail/*`): item info payload assembly, header/detail data updates, licensing status lifecycle and feedback workflows, product user assignments, licensing notifications
- **Art Piece** (`/artPiece/*`): art piece/design library creation, detail retrieval, attachment management, archival/deletion, primary image selection
- **Licensing Tracking** helpers: status assignment, feedback threads, milestone tracking, Teams notification dispatch to assigned users and groups

Source references:
- `README.md` (lines 1-4)
- `docs/system-interconnectivity.md` (Feature Ownership table)
- `docs/system-architecture.md` (Repository Responsibilities, designflow-item-master section, lines 237-252)

---

### 2. Tech Stack

- **Runtime & Framework**: Node.js CommonJS, Express v4.22.1
- **ORM**: Sequelize v6.37.3 (models + schema sync on startup, no dedicated migration runner)
- **Database driver**: PostgreSQL (`pg` v8.11.5) with native Postgres dialect
- **Auth**: RS256 JWT (Designflow JWT validation, not Supabase Auth)
- **Key dependencies**:
  - `@azure/identity`, `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client`, `botframework-connector` — Teams/Graph integration
  - `axios` — HTTP outbound (Teams, Coldlion API calls)
  - `multer` — file upload middleware
  - `imagemagick` — image processing (art piece thumbnails)
  - `bcrypt` — password hashing (legacy, may not be active)
  - `jest`, `@playwright/test` — testing

Source: `package.json` (lines 18-40)

---

### 3. Database Access

**Connection model:**
- Loads `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME` from environment (enforced at startup)
- Connects via Sequelize with PostgreSQL dialect to a **shared schema** (namespace set via `process.env.SCHEMA`)
- Pool size: `max: 22, min: 1, acquire: 600000ms (10 min), idle: 10000ms`
- Connect timeout: 300000ms (5 min)
- No `createdAt`/`updatedAt` timestamps (disabled globally)
- Runs `sequelize.sync()` on startup (idempotent schema alignment, no explicit migration runner)

Source: `config/db.config.js`, `models/db.js` (lines 1-40)

**Sequelize models (34 files):**

Core item family:
- `itemHeader` — primary item record (item_id_pk, item_num_id, item_descr_name, dimensions, uom, created/mod timestamps/user, active/discontinued status, item_type_id_fk)
- `itemDetail` — item detail properties (item_pk, label_code, size/prepack/dim codes, UPC/EAN/GTIN, retail price, cost, 10 merch-group UDF fields)
- `itemType` — item type taxonomy (item_type_id, name, description, images)
- `itemAttachment` — item media (item_num_id_fk FK to itemHeader)

Art piece family:
- `artPiece` — design asset record (id, art_description, licensor/property/style-guide/theme/art-type/source IDs all FK to merchGroup)
- `artPieceAttachment` — design media (art_piece_id FK)

Licensing & feedback:
- `licensingStatus` — licensing assignment record (itemheader_id_fk, assignee_id, assignee_ids JSONB array, status, feedback, JSONB attachments, assignor_id, tagged_group_id FK to groups)
- `licensingMilestone` — milestone tracking
- `LicenseFeedBacks` — feedback option library
- `licensingFeedbackReply` — reply threads (created_by FK to users, licensing_status_id_fk)
- `groups` — user groups (used for team notifications, member_user_ids JSONB array)

Production & order:
- `ProdOrderHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` — production order family (linked by itemNo FK to itemHeader.item_num_id)
- `ProdPaymentTerms`, `ProdShipmentTransitTime`, `ShippingPort`

Taxonomy & metadata:
- `merchGroup` — merch group hierarchy (mg_id, parent_id, mg_code, mg_desc, mgTypeCode, divisionCode_id_fk, is_active, mgCode2, mgCategory)
- `AgeGroup`, `ArtTypes`, `Artist` — enumeration tables
- `ProductCategory` — product category taxonomy

Grid & view:
- `GridLayout`, `GridAccessLevel`, `GridCellNote` — UI grid persistence and cell notes

UDF & configuration:
- `UDFTable` — custom field definitions

Users & shared:
- `users` — system users (id, name, lastname, email)
- `productUserAssignment` — product-user assignment (item_id_fk, user_id_fk)

External integrations:
- `externalCustomer`, `externalVendor` — Coldlion-sync records
- `RFQItem` — RFQ reference (shared with core backend)
- `AuditLog`, `SeasonCode`, `deliveryLocation` — supporting metadata

**Associations defined in init-models.js:**
- itemHeader → itemAttachment (1:M)
- itemHeader → itemType (M:1)
- itemHeader → ProdOrderDetail (1:M as prodOrderDetails)
- artPiece → artPieceAttachment (1:M)
- licensingStatus ↔ users (M:1 assignee, M:1 assignor)
- licensingStatus ↔ groups (M:1 taggedGroup)
- licensingFeedbackReply → licensingStatus (M:1)
- licensingFeedbackReply → users (M:1 author)
- productUserAssignment ↔ itemHeader, users (M:1 each)
- merchGroup hierarchy loops (10 parent-child cycles for merch-group UDF fields)

Source: `models/db/init-models.js` (lines 35-100+); all model files in `models/db/*.js`

**Transaction usage:**
- One identified transaction in art_piece.service.js line 660 (delete art pieces with cascading attachment cleanup)

**Shared models with other services:**
Per `unified-supabase-schema-map.md` (lines 142-165), item-master shares Sequelize model definitions with:
- **designflow-backend**: AgeGroup, ArtTypes, Artist, AuditLog
- **designflow-data-syncing**: Factory, vendor, vendorGroup, externalVendor, itemHeader, itemDetail, itemAttachment, ProdOrderHeader, ProdOrderDetail
- **designflow-tracking**: ProdOrderHeader, ProdOrderDetail, licensingStatus, licensingMilestone, groups

**Migration-critical observation**: the same tables (`itemHeader`, `itemDetail`, etc.) are referenced by **multiple repos' Sequelize models**. Each repo defines its own copy of the model file. A Supabase migration must ensure **one canonical set of columns** across all PLM services, or schema drift will cause ORM mismatches.

---

### 4. Auth

**Mechanism**: RS256 JWT (Designflow JWT, not Supabase Auth)
- Public key loaded from `process.env.JWT_PUBLIC_KEY`
- Token expected in `Authorization` header
- BFF remaps user's `X-User-Authorization` header to `Authorization` before reaching item-master

Source: `helpers/auth.js` (lines 1-10)

**Middleware:**
- `authRole(roles)` — route guard enforcing role membership; role values: `admin`, `sourcing_manager`, `designer`, `vendor`, `sales`, `production`
- `contextMiddleware` — AsyncLocalStorage context injection (user info + request UUID for distributed tracing)

Source: `routes/item_library.router.js` (line 5-8); all routes gated by `authRole`

---

### 5. External Integrations

**Teams/Microsoft Graph integration:**
- Classes: `TeamsService` with Graph Client and Bot Framework Connector
- Environment: `TEAMS_TENANT_ID`, `TEAMS_CLIENT_ID`, `TEAMS_CLIENT_SECRET`, `TEAMS_SERVICE_URL` (defaults to amer traffic manager), `TEAMS_APP_CATALOG_ID` or `TEAMS_APP_ID`
- Usage: sends licensing assignment notifications to user emails and team group chats
- Called by item_detail.service (notifyLicensingAssignees, notifyLicensingTaggedGroup)

Source: `services/teams.service.js` (lines 1-100+)

**Coldlion ERP sync:**
- API key via `COLDLION_API_KEY`
- Referenced in item_detail.service.js for potential ERP data enrichment
- Not directly called by item-master (data-syncing service pulls from Coldlion and writes to shared tables)

Source: `config/coldlion.config.js`; `services/item_detail.service.js` line 15

**DigitalOcean Spaces (file storage):**
- Not directly integrated in item-master code (no Spaces client calls found)
- Hypothesis: image URLs/attachment URLs are stored in the database; presigning/access likely handled by core backend or BFF

**Crons/Scheduled tasks:**
- None identified in this codebase (no cron libraries, no background job queues)

---

### 6. API Surface

**Route shape and domains:**

All routes mounted under `/lib`, `/detail`, `/artPiece`, `/api/health`.

**Item Library (`/lib/*`):**
- `GET /lib/getAllitems` — paginated item grid (startRow, endRow, filterModel, isArchive query params)
- `GET /lib/getApiAllItems` — legacy all-items export
- `GET /lib/getViewlayout/:grid_id` — retrieve grid layout config
- `POST /lib/updateProdOrderHeader` — update production order header
- `POST /lib/createItem` — create new item (generates LIB-xxx number with max 25 collision retries)
- `GET /lib/getItemTypes` — list active item types
- `GET /lib/searchItemTypes` — search item types by name/description
- `POST /lib/createItemType` — add new item type
- `GET /lib/getItemByNumber/:itemNumber` — lookup by item number
- `GET /lib/getItemsByDsnRefNum/:dsnRefNum` — lookup by design reference number
- `POST /lib/getGridCellNotes`, `POST /lib/setGridCellNote` — grid cell note persistence
- `GET /lib/getLicensorsWithProperties` — merch-group hierarchy (licensor parent → properties children, filtered by division)

Source: `routes/item_library.router.js`; `docs/api-reference.md` lines 45-61

**Item Detail (`/detail/*`):**
- `POST /detail/getItemInfo` — full item payload (header, detail, attachments, licensing, assignments)
- `POST /detail/updateData` — bulk update item detail fields
- `POST /detail/setPrimaryImage` — mark item attachment as primary
- `POST /detail/AddLicensingStatus`, `/detail/BulkAddLicensingStatus` — create licensing assignments (triggers Teams notifications)
- `POST /detail/DeleteLicensingStatus` — remove licensing status
- `GET /detail/GetLicensingStatus/:id`, `POST /detail/BulkGetLicensingStatus` — fetch licensing records
- `POST /detail/UpdateLicensingStatus` — modify status, feedback, assignee
- `POST /detail/UpdateLicensingFeedbackReply` — reply to licensing feedback
- `POST /detail/reqStyeNumber` — request style number generation
- `POST /detail/updateItemHeader` — update item header fields
- `GET /detail/GetNotifications` — retrieve item-detail notifications
- `GET /detail/GetLicensingTimeline/:id` — build licensing milestone timeline
- `GET|POST /detail/assignments/:itemId`, `DELETE /detail/assignments/:itemId/:role` — product user assignments
- `POST /detail/getLicenseFeedBacks` — fetch feedback option list
- `GET /detail/GetLicensingFeedbackReplies/:licensingStatusId`, `POST /detail/AddLicensingFeedbackReply` — manage feedback threads

Source: `routes/item_detail.router.js`; `docs/api-reference.md` lines 62-83

**Art Piece (`/artPiece/*`):**
- `GET /artPiece/getAllitems` — paginated art piece grid
- `GET /artPiece/getArtPiece/:id` — detail
- `GET /artPiece/getViewlayout/:grid_id` — grid layout
- `POST /artPiece/createItem` — create art piece
- `POST /artPiece/setPrimaryImage` — set primary image
- `POST /artPiece/addAttachment/:id` — add attachment (e.g., style guide file)
- `POST /artPiece/updateArtPiece` — modify art piece
- `GET /artPiece/getProductCategories` — product category dropdown
- `POST /artPiece/updateArchive` — archive/unarchive
- `POST /artPiece/deleteArtPieces` — delete (with eligibility check)
- `POST /artPiece/checkDeleteEligibility` — pre-delete validation
- `POST /artPiece/getGridCellNotes`, `POST /artPiece/setGridCellNote` — grid cell notes

Source: `routes/art_piece.router.js`; `docs/api-reference.md` lines 84-101

**Health:**
- `GET /api/health` — service availability check (no auth required)

Source: `routes/health.router.js`

**Callers:**
- Frontend Angular modules: ItemLibraryService, ItemDetailService, ArtPieceService
- BFF route prefix: `/api/item_master/*`
- Deployed base: `https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/*`

Source: `docs/api-reference.md` (lines 1-37)

---

### 7. Heavy Business Logic (App-code Responsibilities)

**Item number generation** (services/item_library.service.js):
- Generates `LIB-<guid>` or similar item numbers
- **Collision avoidance**: max 25 attempts to find unique value in itemHeader.item_num_id **and** RFQItem.rfqItem_style_number
- **Does NOT loop unbounded** (bounded retry prevents hammer-load on the database)
- Stores decision in unit test: `tests/unit/item-number-generation.test.js`
- **Migration note**: this logic must remain in application code; no move to Supabase RLS/functions

Source: `docs/system-architecture.md` lines 471-479; `services/item_library.service.js` lines 16-17

**Item type search with scoring** (services/item_library.service.js):
- Parses search query into terms
- Case-insensitive ILIKE match on item_type_name and item_type_description
- Scores and ranks candidates (utility.scoreItemTypeCandidate helper)
- Limit 8 results

**Licensor-property hierarchy mapping** (services/item_library.service.js lines 69-136):
- Queries merchGroup by mgTypeCode='05' (licensor) and '06' (property)
- Filters by division code if supplied
- Maps hierarchies (parent_id relationship)
- Returns licensors with nested properties array

**Licensing status lifecycle** (services/item_detail.service.js):
- Add/update/delete licensing records with user assignment
- **Triggers Teams notifications** to assignees (individual emails) and tagged groups (group chat)
- Attachments stored as JSONB in licensingStatus.attachments
- Feedback replies linked via licensingFeedbackReply.licensing_status_id_fk
- Builds licensing timeline (milestones, transitions) using buildItemLicensingTimeline helper

Source: `services/item_detail.service.js` lines 62-100+

**Product-user assignment** (services/product_assignment.service.js):
- Manages productUserAssignment table (item_id_fk → user_id_fk)
- Used for product manager/owner designation

**Item deletion cascades** (services/art_piece.service.js line 660):
- Uses Sequelize transaction to delete artPiece + cascading artPieceAttachments
- Eligibility check before deletion

**Audit logging** (services/audit.service.js):
- Logs record creation/modification to AuditLog table

**Heavy computational steps:**
- Grid layout retrieval with AG Grid-compatible row/column payloads
- Bulk licensing status operations (batch create, batch get)
- Notification dispatch (Teams API calls within request path — **synchronous, blocking**)

---

### 8. Migration-Relevant Observations

**GCP coupling:**
- No explicit GCP SDK imports (Cloud SQL is accessed via standard `pg` driver)
- Sequelize pool config uses millisecond timeouts suitable for managed cloud DBs
- No socket-based connection (host/port model, not Cloud SQL Unix socket)
- Can be migrated to any PostgreSQL-compatible database without code change (driver, connection string only)

Source: `config/db.config.js`; `models/db.js` line 28

**Environment variables critical to migration:**
- `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME` — standard Postgres creds
- `SCHEMA` — Supabase logical schema namespace (e.g., `plm`)
- `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` — Designflow JWT validation (must remain, Supabase Auth NOT required for item-master)
- `TEAMS_*` — Teams integration (optional but used if present)
- `COLDLION_API_KEY` — ERP sync (used by data-syncing, referenced but not directly called by item-master)
- `PORT`, `WEB_URL`, `API_ADDRESS` — app metadata

**Model duplication hazard:**
Item-master shares Sequelize model definitions with designflow-backend and designflow-tracking. Per `unified-supabase-schema-map.md` line 6: *"PLM model duplication across repos means a model name alone is not enough. Diff columns by repo before writing PLM DDL."*

Risk: If one repo's model has a column that another repo's model lacks, schema drift breaks the ORM. **Supabase DDL must define the union of all columns across all repos' Sequelize definitions**, or item-master may fail to access columns it expects.

Source: `shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md` line 86

**What breaks if DB moves:**
1. If Supabase SCHEMA is different from GCP, all model tableName references must match or Sequelize won't find tables.
2. If SCHEMA is `plm` but models reference `public`, queries fail.
3. If columns are dropped that any repo's model references, ORM errors.
4. If column types change (e.g., JSONB → string), casting logic breaks.
5. If pool size/timeouts are too small, concurrent requests timeout.

**Other services write to item-master tables:**
- **designflow-data-syncing**: writes itemHeader, itemDetail, ProdOrderHeader/Detail, externalVendor/Customer (Coldlion sync)
- **designflow-tracking**: reads and writes licensingStatus, licensingMilestone, ProdOrderHeader/Detail, groups, reads itemHeader
- **designflow-backend**: core RFQ reads itemHeader, itemType, vendor/customer, merch-group; shared users, grid layouts

**No transactions required at the database level** except the one art-piece delete. Most operations are single-table writes. Licensing notifications are sent **synchronously within the request handler** — Teams API call blocks response. This is a performance risk if Teams API is slow; Supabase migration does not change this behavior (it's application-level, not DB-level).

**Schema changes needed for Supabase:**
Per shared-db planning docs, item-master tables will move to `plm` schema (not `public`). All model definitions must reference the same schema consistently. The Supabase migration package (`supabase/migrations/20260621000*.sql`) should add `plm.item`, `plm.item_detail`, `plm.item_attachment`, `plm.art_piece`, `plm.licensing_status` as target tables.

Source: `shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md` lines 73-75; `shared-db/README.md` line 39

**No breaking changes required for item-master application code:**
- No Socket-only connection → TCP/IP socket can work for Supabase
- No GCP-specific calls (Pub/Sub, Cloud Storage, IAM APIs)
- No direct Cloud SQL proxy auth
- Connection pool and timeout configs are safe for managed Postgres
- JWT validation already Designflow-scoped (does not require Supabase Auth change)

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Service** | designflow-item-master (port 5003, Cloud Run private) |
| **Responsibility** | Item Library, Item Detail, Art Piece, Licensing Status workflows |
| **Tech Stack** | Node.js, Express, Sequelize v6.37.3, PostgreSQL (`pg` driver) |
| **Models** | 34 Sequelize model files; item family shared with backend/tracking/sync |
| **Auth** | RS256 JWT (X-User-Authorization → Authorization) |
| **DB Config** | Host/port TCP (not socket), pool max 22, idle 10s, acquire timeout 600s |
| **Transactions** | One identified (art piece delete cascade) |
| **Integrations** | Teams (notifications), Coldlion API key (via data-sync), DigitalOcean Spaces (URLs stored, not accessed) |
| **Routes** | 40+ endpoints across /lib, /detail, /artPiece, /api/health |
| **Heavy Logic** | Item-number generation (bounded retry), licensing workflows, Teams notifications (blocking) |
| **GCP Coupling** | None; standard Postgres TCP connection only |
| **Migration Risks** | Model duplication across repos; schema namespace mismatch; column type/presence drift |
| **Supabase-Ready** | Yes; add to `plm` schema, maintain column parity across repos, preserve env vars, no code changes required |

---

All file paths are absolute and relative to `C:/repos/.dflow-audit/designflow-item-master/`.

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive migration analysis report.

## Item Master Service — Supabase GCP-to-Hosted Migration Audit

---

### 1. Purpose & Responsibility

**designflow-item-master** is a dedicated Node.js service (port 5003, Cloud Run private) that manages the PLM item and design master data workflows. It is not part of the informal "five repo" description but is wired into the frontend through the BFF at `/api/item_master/*`.

**Primary domains owned:**
- **Item Library** (`/lib/*`): item creation, retrieval, item type management, item number generation with collision checking, grid layout persistence
- **Item Detail** (`/detail/*`): item info payload assembly, header/detail data updates, licensing status lifecycle and feedback workflows, product user assignments, licensing notifications
- **Art Piece** (`/artPiece/*`): art piece/design library creation, detail retrieval, attachment management, archival/deletion, primary image selection
- **Licensing Tracking** helpers: status assignment, feedback threads, milestone tracking, Teams notification dispatch to assigned users and groups

Source references:
- `README.md` (lines 1-4)
- `docs/system-interconnectivity.md` (Feature Ownership table)
- `docs/system-architecture.md` (Repository Responsibilities, designflow-item-master section, lines 237-252)

---

### 2. Tech Stack

- **Runtime & Framework**: Node.js CommonJS, Express v4.22.1
- **ORM**: Sequelize v6.37.3 (models + schema sync on startup, no dedicated migration runner)
- **Database driver**: PostgreSQL (`pg` v8.11.5) with native Postgres dialect
- **Auth**: RS256 JWT (Designflow JWT validation, not Supabase Auth)
- **Key dependencies**:
  - `@azure/identity`, `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client`, `botframework-connector` — Teams/Graph integration
  - `axios` — HTTP outbound (Teams, Coldlion API calls)
  - `multer` — file upload middleware
  - `imagemagick` — image processing (art piece thumbnails)
  - `bcrypt` — password hashing (legacy, may not be active)
  - `jest`, `@playwright/test` — testing

Source: `package.json` (lines 18-40)

---

### 3. Database Access

**Connection model:**
- Loads `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME` from environment (enforced at startup)
- Connects via Sequelize with PostgreSQL dialect to a **shared schema** (namespace set via `process.env.SCHEMA`)
- Pool size: `max: 22, min: 1, acquire: 600000ms (10 min), idle: 10000ms`
- Connect timeout: 300000ms (5 min)
- No `createdAt`/`updatedAt` timestamps (disabled globally)
- Runs `sequelize.sync()` on startup (idempotent schema alignment, no explicit migration runner)

Source: `config/db.config.js`, `models/db.js` (lines 1-40)

**Sequelize models (34 files):**

Core item family:
- `itemHeader` — primary item record (item_id_pk, item_num_id, item_descr_name, dimensions, uom, created/mod timestamps/user, active/discontinued status, item_type_id_fk)

- `itemAttachment` — item media (item_num_id_fk FK to itemHeader)

Art piece family:
- `artPiece` — design asset record (id, art_description, licensor/property/style-guide/theme/art-type/source IDs all FK to merchGroup)

Licensing & feedback:
- `licensingStatus` — licensing assignment record (itemheader_id_fk, assignee_id, assignee_ids JSONB array, status, feedback, JSONB attachments, assignor_id, tagged_group_id FK to groups)
- `licensingMilestone` — milestone tracking
- `LicenseFeedBacks` — feedback option library
- `licensingFeedbackReply` — reply threads (created_by FK to users, licensing_status_id_fk)
- `groups` — user groups (used for team notifications, member_user_ids JSONB array)

Production & order:
- `ProdOrderHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` — production order family (linked by itemNo FK to itemHeader.item_num_id)
- `ProdPaymentTerms`, `ProdShipmentTransitTime`, `ShippingPort`

Taxonomy & metadata:
- `merchGroup` — merch group hierarchy (mg_id, parent_id, mg_code, mg_desc, mgTypeCode, divisionCode_id_fk, is_active, mgCode2, mgCategory)
- `AgeGroup`, `ArtTypes`, `Artist` — enumeration tables
- `ProductCategory` — product category taxonomy

Grid & view:
- `GridLayout`, `GridAccessLevel`, `GridCellNote` — UI grid persistence and cell notes

UDF & configuration:
- `UDFTable` — custom field definitions

Users & shared:
- `users` — system users (id, name, lastname, email)
- `productUserAssignment` — product-user assignment (item_id_fk, user_id_fk)

External integrations:
- `externalCustomer`, `externalVendor` — Coldlion-sync records
- `RFQItem` — RFQ reference (shared with core backend)
- `AuditLog`, `SeasonCode`, `deliveryLocation` — supporting metadata

**Associations defined in init-models.js:**
- itemHeader → itemAttachment (1:M)
- itemHeader → itemType (M:1)
- itemHeader → ProdOrderDetail (1:M as prodOrderDetails)
- artPiece → artPieceAttachment (1:M)
- licensingStatus ↔ users (M:1 assignee, M:1 assignor)
- licensingStatus ↔ groups (M:1 taggedGroup)
- licensingFeedbackReply → licensingStatus (M:1)

- productUserAssignment ↔ itemHeader, users (M:1 each)
- merchGroup hierarchy loops (10 parent-child cycles for merch-group UDF fields)

Source: `models/db/init-models.js` (lines 35-100+); all model files in `models/db/*.js`

**Transaction usage:**
- One identified transaction in art_piece.service.js line 660 (delete art pieces with cascading attachment cleanup)

**Shared models with other services:**
Per `unified-supabase-schema-map.md` (lines 142-165), item-master shares Sequelize model definitions with:

- **designflow-data-syncing**: Factory, vendor, vendorGroup, externalVendor, itemHeader, itemDetail, itemAttachment, ProdOrderHeader, ProdOrderDetail

**Migration-critical observation**: the same tables (`itemHeader`, `itemDetail`, etc.) are referenced by **multiple repos' Sequelize models**. Each repo defines its own copy of the model file. A Supabase migration must ensure **one canonical set of columns** across all PLM services, or schema drift will cause ORM mismatches.

**Teams/Microsoft Graph integration:**

- Environment: `TEAMS_TENANT_ID`, `TEAMS_CLIENT_ID`, `TEAMS_CLIENT_SECRET`, `TEAMS_SERVICE_URL` (defaults to amer traffic manager), `TEAMS_APP_CATALOG_ID` or `TEAMS_APP_ID`

**Crons/Scheduled tasks:**
- None identified in this codebase (no cron libraries, no background job queues)

---

### 6. API Surface

**Route shape and domains:**

All routes mounted under `/lib`, `/detail`, `/artPiece`, `/api/health`.

**Item Library (`/lib/*`):**
- `GET /lib/getAllitems` — paginated item grid (startRow, endRow, filterModel, isArchive query params)
- `GET /lib/getApiAllItems` — legacy all-items export
- `GET /lib/getViewlayout/:grid_id` — retrieve grid layout config
- `POST /lib/updateProdOrderHeader` — update production order header
- `POST /lib/createItem` — create new item (generates LIB-xxx number with max 25 collision retries)
- `GET /lib/getItemTypes` — list active item types
- `GET /lib/searchItemTypes` — search item types by name/description
- `POST /lib/createItemType` — add new item type
- `GET /lib/getItemByNumber/:itemNumber` — lookup by item number
- `GET /lib/getItemsByDsnRefNum/:dsnRefNum` — lookup by design reference number
- `POST /lib/getGridCellNotes`, `POST /lib/setGridCellNote` — grid cell note persistence
- `GET /lib/getLicensorsWithProperties` — merch-group hierarchy (licensor parent → properties children, filtered by division)

Source: `routes/item_library.router.js`; `docs/api-reference.md` lines 45-61

**Item Detail (`/detail/*`):**
- `POST /detail/getItemInfo` — full item payload (header, detail, attachments, licensing, assignments)
- `POST /detail/updateData` — bulk update item detail fields
- `POST /detail/setPrimaryImage` — mark item attachment as primary
- `POST /detail/AddLicensingStatus`, `/detail/BulkAddLicensingStatus` — create licensing assignments (triggers Teams notifications)
- `POST /detail/DeleteLicensingStatus` — remove licensing status
- `GET /detail/GetLicensingStatus/:id`, `POST /detail/BulkGetLicensingStatus` — fetch licensing records
- `POST /detail/UpdateLicensingStatus` — modify status, feedback, assignee
- `POST /detail/UpdateLicensingFeedbackReply` — reply to licensing feedback
- `POST /detail/reqStyeNumber` — request style number generation
- `POST /detail/updateItemHeader` — update item header fields
- `GET /detail/GetNotifications` — retrieve item-detail notifications
- `GET /detail/GetLicensingTimeline/:id` — build licensing milestone timeline
- `GET|POST /detail/assignments/:itemId`, `DELETE /detail/assignments/:itemId/:role` — product user assignments
- `POST /detail/getLicenseFeedBacks` — fetch feedback option list
- `GET /detail/GetLicensingFeedbackReplies/:licensingStatusId`, `POST /detail/AddLicensingFeedbackReply` — manage feedback threads

Source: `routes/item_detail.router.js`; `docs/api-reference.md` lines 62-83

**Art Piece (`/artPiece/*`):**
- `GET /artPiece/getAllitems` — paginated art piece grid
- `GET /artPiece/getArtPiece/:id` — detail
- `GET /artPiece/getViewlayout/:grid_id` — grid layout
- `POST /artPiece/createItem` — create art piece
- `POST /artPiece/setPrimaryImage` — set primary image
- `POST /artPiece/addAttachment/:id` — add attachment (e.g., style guide file)
- `POST /artPiece/updateArtPiece` — modify art piece
- `GET /artPiece/getProductCategories` — product category dropdown
- `POST /artPiece/updateArchive` — archive/unarchive
- `POST /artPiece/deleteArtPieces` — delete (with eligibility check)
- `POST /artPiece/checkDeleteEligibility` — pre-delete validation
- `POST /artPiece/getGridCellNotes`, `POST /artPiece/setGridCellNote` — grid cell notes

Source: `routes/art_piece.router.js`; `docs/api-reference.md` lines 84-101

**Health:**
- `GET /api/health` — service availability check (no auth required)

Source: `routes/health.router.js`

**Callers:**
- Frontend Angular modules: ItemLibraryService, ItemDetailService, ArtPieceService
- BFF route prefix: `/api/item_master/*`
- Deployed base: `https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/*`

Source: `docs/api-reference.md` (lines 1-37)

---

### 7. Heavy Business Logic (App-code Responsibilities)

**Item number generation** (services/item_library.service.js):
- Generates `LIB-<guid>` or similar item numbers
- **Collision avoidance**: max 25 attempts to find unique value in itemHeader.item_num_id **and** RFQItem.rfqItem_style_number
- **Does NOT loop unbounded** (bounded retry prevents hammer-load on the database)
- Stores decision in unit test: `tests/unit/item-number-generation.test.js`
- **Migration note**: this logic must remain in application code; no move to Supabase RLS/functions

Source: `docs/system-architecture.md` lines 471-479; `services/item_library.service.js` lines 16-17

**Item type search with scoring** (services/item_library.service.js):
- Parses search query into terms
- Case-insensitive ILIKE match on item_type_name and item_type_description
- Scores and ranks candidates (utility.scoreItemTypeCandidate helper)
- Limit 8 results

**Licensor-property hierarchy mapping** (services/item_library.service.js lines 69-136):
- Queries merchGroup by mgTypeCode='05' (licensor) and '06' (property)
- Filters by division code if supplied
- Maps hierarchies (parent_id relationship)
- Returns licensors with nested properties array

**Licensing status lifecycle** (services/item_detail.service.js):
- Add/update/delete licensing records with user assignment
- **Triggers Teams notifications** to assignees (individual emails) and tagged groups (group chat)
- Attachments stored as JSONB in licensingStatus.attachments
- Feedback replies linked via licensingFeedbackReply.licensing_status_id_fk
- Builds licensing timeline (milestones, transitions) using buildItemLicensingTimeline helper

Source: `services/item_detail.service.js` lines 62-100+

**Product-user assignment** (services/product_assignment.service.js):
- Manages productUserAssignment table (item_id_fk → user_id_fk)
- Used for product manager/owner designation

**Item deletion cascades** (services/art_piece.service.js line 660):
- Uses Sequelize transaction to delete artPiece + cascading artPieceAttachments
- Eligibility check before deletion

**Audit logging** (services/audit.service.js):
- Logs record creation/modification to AuditLog table

**Heavy computational steps:**
- Grid layout retrieval with AG Grid-compatible row/column payloads
- Bulk licensing status operations (batch create, batch get)
- Notification dispatch (Teams API calls within request path — **synchronous, blocking**)

---

### 8. Migration-Relevant Observations

**GCP coupling:**
- No explicit GCP SDK imports (Cloud SQL is accessed via standard `pg` driver)
- Sequelize pool config uses millisecond timeouts suitable for managed cloud DBs
- No socket-based connection (host/port model, not Cloud SQL Unix socket)
- Can be migrated to any PostgreSQL-compatible database without code change (driver, connection string only)

Source: `config/db.config.js`; `models/db.js` line 28

**Environment variables critical to migration:**
- `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME` — standard Postgres creds
- `SCHEMA` — Supabase logical schema namespace (e.g., `plm`)
- `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` — Designflow JWT validation (must remain, Supabase Auth NOT required for item-master)
- `TEAMS_*` — Teams integration (optional but used if present)
- `COLDLION_API_KEY` — ERP sync (used by data-syncing, referenced but not directly called by item-master)
- `PORT`, `WEB_URL`, `API_ADDRESS` — app metadata

**Model duplication hazard:**
Item-master shares Sequelize model definitions with designflow-backend and designflow-tracking. Per `unified-supabase-schema-map.md` line 6: *"PLM model duplication across repos means a model name alone is not enough. Diff columns by repo before writing PLM DDL."*

Risk: If one repo's model has a column that another repo's model lacks, schema drift breaks the ORM. **Supabase DDL must define the union of all columns across all repos' Sequelize definitions**, or item-master may fail to access columns it expects.

Source: `shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md` line 86

**What breaks if DB moves:**
1. If Supabase SCHEMA is different from GCP, all model tableName references must match or Sequelize won't find tables.
2. If SCHEMA is `plm` but models reference `public`, queries fail.
3. If columns are dropped that any repo's model references, ORM errors.
4. If column types change (e.g., JSONB → string), casting logic breaks.
5. If pool size/timeouts are too small, concurrent requests timeout.

**Other services write to item-master tables:**
- **designflow-data-syncing**: writes itemHeader, itemDetail, ProdOrderHeader/Detail, externalVendor/Customer (Coldlion sync)
- **designflow-tracking**: reads and writes licensingStatus, licensingMilestone, ProdOrderHeader/Detail, groups, reads itemHeader
- **designflow-backend**: core RFQ reads itemHeader, itemType, vendor/customer, merch-group; shared users, grid layouts

**No transactions required at the database level** except the one art-piece delete. Most operations are single-table writes. Licensing notifications are sent **synchronously within the request handler** — Teams API call blocks response. This is a performance risk if Teams API is slow; Supabase migration does not change this behavior (it's application-level, not DB-level).

**Schema changes needed for Supabase:**
Per shared-db planning docs, item-master tables will move to `plm` schema (not `public`). All model definitions must reference the same schema consistently. The Supabase migration package (`supabase/migrations/20260621000*.sql`) should add `plm.item`, `plm.item_detail`, `plm.item_attachment`, `plm.art_piece`, `plm.licensing_status` as target tables.

Source: `shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md` lines 73-75; `shared-db/README.md` line 39

**No breaking changes required for item-master application code:**
- No Socket-only connection → TCP/IP socket can work for Supabase
- No GCP-specific calls (Pub/Sub, Cloud Storage, IAM APIs)
- No direct Cloud SQL proxy auth
- Connection pool and timeout configs are safe for managed Postgres
- JWT validation already Designflow-scoped (does not require Supabase Auth change)

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Service** | designflow-item-master (port 5003, Cloud Run private) |
| **Responsibility** | Item Library, Item Detail, Art Piece, Licensing Status workflows |
| **Tech Stack** | Node.js, Express, Sequelize v6.37.3, PostgreSQL (`pg` driver) |
| **Models** | 34 Sequelize model files; item family shared with backend/tracking/sync |
| **Auth** | RS256 JWT (X-User-Authorization → Authorization) |
| **DB Config** | Host/port TCP (not socket), pool max 22, idle 10s, acquire timeout 600s |
| **Transactions** | One identified (art piece delete cascade) |
| **Integrations** | Teams (notifications), Coldlion API key (via data-sync), DigitalOcean Spaces (URLs stored, not accessed) |
| **Routes** | 40+ endpoints across /lib, /detail, /artPiece, /api/health |
| **Heavy Logic** | Item-number generation (bounded retry), licensing workflows, Teams notifications (blocking) |
| **GCP Coupling** | None; standard Postgres TCP connection only |
| **Migration Risks** | Model duplication across repos; schema namespace mismatch; column type/presence drift |
| **Supabase-Ready** | Yes; add to `plm` schema, maintain column parity across repos, preserve env vars, no code changes required |

---

All file paths are absolute and relative to `C:/repos/.dflow-audit/designflow-item-master/`.

aiTitle: Resolve uncommitted changes in supabase pooler docs

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive knowledge of the service. Let me compile the structured analysis report.

---

## PLM Data-Syncing Service Analysis: GCP Cloud SQL to Supabase Migration

### 1. **Purpose & Responsibility**

The `designflow-data-syncing` service is a **private system-to-system ETL/integration layer** that acts as the Coldlion → Designflow synchronization boundary. It is **not user-facing**; every request requires `X-API-Key` authentication.

**Data flows:**
- **Source (Coldlion ERP)** → `designflow-data-syncing` → **Destination (shared PLM PostgreSQL schema)**
- Pulls master data from Coldlion API (external inventory/ERP system) via paginated HTTP calls
- Upserts into the shared Designflow database schema (same schema used by `designflow-backend`, `designflow-item-master`, `designflow-tracking`)
- Downstream consumers (item-master, backend, tracking services) read the synced data

**Primary sync domains:**
- **Item master**: item headers, item details, item attachments (images uploaded to GCS)
- **Merch groups**: merchandising hierarchy headers and details
- **Production orders**: order headers and details
- **External references**: vendor and customer records
- **Prepack associations**: production order detail ↔ item mappings

**Responsibility scope** (per `AGENTS.md` and `system-architecture.md`):
- Fetch paginated Coldlion data over HTTPS with `COLDLION_API_KEY`
- Transform/remap Coldlion field names to internal DB columns (`helpers/utility.js`)
- Find-or-create and upsert rows with `modTime`-based freshness guards
- Sync streaming for merch group detail with Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- Bulk upsert production orders and prepack associations

---

### 2. **Tech Stack**

- **Runtime**: Node.js LTS (CommonJS, no TypeScript)
- **Framework**: Express.js
- **ORM**: Sequelize v6 (models-based, no separate migration tool)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL via `pg` v8
- **HTTP client**: Axios v1
- **Server-side job scheduler**: None (API-driven sync, no cron/scheduled jobs in this repo; host-level systemd timers invoke endpoints)
- **Testing**: Jest (unit tests only); Playwright boilerplate (not wired to service)
- **Container**: Docker (`node:lts-alpine`), built via Cloud Build
- **Image processing**: ImageMagick (installed in Docker for image resize/conversion)
- **File upload utility**: Multer for multipart/form-data handling

**Key dependencies** in `package.json`:
```json
"axios": "^1.13.5",
"express": "^4.22.1",
"pg": "^8.11.5",
"sequelize": "^6.37.3",
"dotenv": "^17.3.1",
"jest": "^29.7.0"
```

---

### 3. **Database Access**

**Connection source:** `models/db.js` initializes a **single shared Sequelize instance** to one PostgreSQL database.

**Configuration** (`config/db.config.js`):
- Reads from env: `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`
- Throws if any required var is missing
- No built-in fallback to local/socket connection

**Sequelize pool** (`models/db.js`):
```javascript
pool: {
  max: 22,           // max connections
  min: 1,            // min connections
  acquire: 600000,   // 10-min acquire timeout
  idle: 10000        // 10-sec idle timeout
}
```

**Key dialect options:**
```javascript
dialectOptions: {
  connectTimeout: 300000,  // 5-min initial connect timeout
  client_encoding: 'UTF8'  // Force UTF-8 wire encoding (defense against CP437 mojibake)
}
```

**Schema selection:** `process.env.SCHEMA` (e.g., `designflow_dev`, `designflow`) selected at connect time; Sequelize models all target `sequelize.define(..., { schema: process.env.SCHEMA })`.

**Sync patterns:**
- **Find-or-create**: Uses composite keys (`mgTypeCode + mg_desc + ItemNoCode + divisionCode_fk`, etc.) scoped to division/company
- **Upsert with `modTime` guard**: Bulk inserts with `ignoreDuplicates: true`, then per-row updates only if `modTime` is older (`[Op.lt]`)
- **Bulk operations**: `bulkCreate()` with `ignoreDuplicates`, followed by per-row `update()` loops with `modTime` filtering
- **Transactions**: None explicitly used; each sync function is atomic at the Sequelize level (no explicit `transaction()` wrapper)

**Migrations:**
- No separate migration tool (e.g., Sequelize CLI migrations, Knex, Flyway)
- Startup: `sequelize.sync()` creates missing tables from model definitions
- Column additions: Idempotent inline `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` in `models/db.js` (see `AGENTS.md` §11 for mixed-case identifier quoting requirement)

**Model files** (`models/db/*.js`, physically duplicated from `designflow-backend`):
- `itemHeader.js`, `itemDetail.js`, `itemAttachment.js` — item master tables
- `merchGroup.js`, `merchGroupHeaders.js` — merch hierarchy
- `ProdOrderHeader.js`, `ProdOrderDetail.js` — production orders
- `externalVendor.js`, `externalCustomer.js` — external vendor/customer records (PKs: `vendorCode`, `customerCode`)
- `item_prod_order_detail_associations.js` — prepack-association bridge table

---

### 4. **Sync Flows**

All sync is **pull-based**: the service fetches from Coldlion, not the reverse. Each route is stateless and idempotent (upsert-safe).

| Route | Source | Destination | Frequency | Key table(s) | Dedup/upsert key | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `GET /getItemHeaderFromCL?id=1` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/itemheader?page=N&sort=createdtime,desc` | `itemHeader` | Manual (Saturday check for mod date) | `itemHeader` | Composite: `item_num_id` | Paginated fetch; remaps 50+ Coldlion fields via `utility.remapItemHeader()` |
| `GET /getItemDetailFromCL` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/itemDetails` | `itemDetail` | Manual | `itemDetail` | `item_pk` (Sequelize primary key) | Remaps 40+ Coldlion fields via `utility.remapItemDetail()` |
| `POST /getItem` | — | `itemHeader` + associations | Manual | `itemHeader`, `itemAttachment` | N/A | Returns item info with attachments; no upsert |
| `GET /getMerchgroupDetailFromCL?companyCode=EDGEHOME&mgTypeCodes=01,02,...&divisionCodes=CW001,...` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/merchGroupDetails?companyCode=...&mgTypeCode=...&divisionCode=...` | `merchGroup` | Manual | `merchGroup` | Composite: `mgTypeCode` + `mg_desc` + `mg_code` + `ItemNoCode` + `divisionCode_fk` + `companyCode_fk` | Loops over divisions (CW001, SP001, EH001) and merch types (01–10); remaps via `utility.remapMGDetail()` |
| `POST /streamMerchgroupDetailFromCL` (body: `{companyCode, mgTypeCode, divisionCode}`) | Coldlion `/EhpApi/merchGroupDetails` | `merchGroup` | Manual (SSE streaming) | `merchGroup` | Same composite key | Uses Server-Sent Events for real-time progress feedback; finds-or-creates with `modTime` ordering |
| `GET /getMerchGroupHeaders` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/merchGroupHeaders?companyCode=EDGEHOME&divisionCode=EH001` | `merchGroupHeaders` | Manual | `merchGroupHeaders` | N/A | Fetches headers; find-or-create |
| `GET /getProdOrderHeader` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/prodtracking?createdFrom=<3 days ago>` | `ProdOrderHeader` | Manual (filter: last 3 days) | `ProdOrderHeader` | N/A | `bulkCreate(..., {ignoreDuplicates: true})`; no additional upsert |
| `GET /getProdOrderDetail` | Loops all `ProdOrderHeader.prodOrderNo`, fetches `/EhpApi/proddetails?prodOrderNo=...` | `ProdOrderDetail` | Manual (full scan of existing headers) | `ProdOrderDetail` | `pkey` | Per-row update if stored `modTime < incoming modTime` |
| `GET /getExternalVendor` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/vendors?createdFrom=<today>&size=2000` | `externalVendor` | Manual (last 24h) | `externalVendor` | `vendorCode` (PK) | `bulkCreate()` + per-row `update()` with `modTime` guard |
| `GET /getExternalCustomer` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/customers?createdFrom=<today>&size=2000` | `externalCustomer` | Manual (last 24h) | `externalCustomer` | `customerCode` (PK) | Same pattern as vendors |
| `GET /syncPrepackAssociations` | Reads all `ProdOrderDetail` where `prepackCode != null`, calls `/EhpApi/prepackDetail?prepackCode=...`; also reads direct items | Coldlion + bridge table `item_prod_order_detail_associations` | Manual | `item_prod_order_detail_associations`, `itemHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` | Composite: `item_header_id` + `prod_order_detail_pkey` + `match_source` | For each prepack code, fetches subitems and links to matched `itemHeader` rows; skips rows already processed (subqueries to check if already in association table) |

**Notable patterns:**
- **Company code mapping** (hardcoded in `utility.js`): `EDGEHOME → 1`, else `→ 2`
- **Division code mapping** (hardcoded in `utility.js`): `CW001 → 1`, `SP001 → 8`, `EH001 → 9`
- **`modTime` freshness**: All upserts check incoming `modTime` against stored `modTime`; only updates if incoming is **newer** (`Op.lt` condition)
- **Pagination**: Item headers paginated at source; merch group detail not paginated (assumes size ≤ API limit)
- **Error handling**: Widely swallowed (axios `.catch()` blocks just `console.log()` and return `undefined`); callers proceed with empty data (see `AGENTS.md` §11)

---

### 5. **Auth**

**Inbound (request auth):**
- All routes protected by `X-API-Key: <DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY>` middleware in `index.js`
- Health check also requires the key (no exception)
- Invalid key → HTTP 500 "not valid key"
- Key stored in `process.env.DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY` (injected from Secret Manager in Cloud Run)

**Outbound (Coldlion calls):**
- Every axios request to Coldlion includes `headers: { "X-API-Key": process.env.COLDLION_API_KEY }`
- Coldlion API base: `http://x5.coldlion.com/EhpApi/...` (hardcoded in most routes, fetched from `externalApi` table in one case)
- Key stored in `process.env.COLDLION_API_KEY` (injected from Secret Manager)

**Service-to-service (in deployment):**
- BFF invokes data-syncing via Cloud Run OIDC token + API key
- No inter-service user-identity propagation (this is a system service, not user-facing)

---

### 6. **External Integrations**

| System | Endpoint | Protocol | Purpose | Credentials |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Coldlion** | `http://x5.coldlion.com/EhpApi/*` | HTTPS (Axios) | Pull item, merch group, prod order, vendor, customer, prepack data | `COLDLION_API_KEY` header |
| **GCS (Google Cloud Storage)** | `https://storage.googleapis.com/` | HTTPS | Upload item images/thumbnails (from sync) | Service account (implicit in Cloud Run) |
| **DigitalOcean Spaces** | N/A (not used in this service) | — | — | — |
| **Directus** | N/A (shared DB is Postgres, not Directus API) | — | — | — |

**Image handling (GCS coupling):**
- Item image sync (`/getItemHeaderFromCL?id=5`) uploads images to `gs://designflow-default-bucket/` via a self-call to `http://localhost:5001/uploadToGCS`
- Images stored locally in `./images/` and `./thumbs/` directories, then uploaded to GCS
- GCS URLs hardcoded to `https://storage.googleapis.com/designflow-default-bucket/` (later rewritten to `https://storage.cloud.google.com/`)
- No fallback storage; if GCS upload fails, a default "No_Image.jpg" is used
- Default image: `https://storage.googleapis.com/designflow-default-bucket/No_Image.jpg`

---

### 7. **Heavy Business Logic**

**Transformation/remapping** (`helpers/utility.js`):
- `remapItemHeader()`: Maps 50+ Coldlion item fields → internal item columns (e.g., `itemDesc` → `item_descr_name`, `itemCost` → `item_cost_ext`). Includes hardcoded company/division code mappings.
- `remapMGDetail()`: Maps merch group fields → internal columns; includes hardcoded division → `divisionCode_id_fk` map.
- `remapItemDetail()`: Maps 40+ item detail fields (UPC, EAN, GTIN, sizes, prices, UDFs, etc.)
- **All remapping includes field-level transformations** (e.g., boolean flags, code→ID mappings, null defaults for unimplemented fields).

**Deduplication/reconciliation** (`models/lib.model.js`):
- **Find-or-create by composite key**: For merch groups, checks all of `mgTypeCode`, `mg_desc`, `mg_code`, `ItemNoCode`, `divisionCode_fk`, `companyCode_fk` before deciding insert vs. update.
- **Freshness guard (`modTime`)**: Every update only proceeds if incoming `modTime > stored modTime`. Uses SQL `WHERE modTime < incoming` to prevent stale overwrites.
- **Bulk insert + per-row update pattern**: For performance, `bulkCreate(…, {ignoreDuplicates: true})` inserts new rows in one batch, then loops through all rows to update existing ones if fresher. Not a single upsert operation.

**Prepack association logic** (`Customer.syncPrepackAssociations()`):
- Groups `ProdOrderDetail` rows by `prepackCode`
- For each prepack, fetches `/EhpApi/prepackDetail?prepackCode=...` to get sub-items
- For each sub-item, finds matching `itemHeader` by `item_num_id` (normalized, cached to avoid repeated lookups)
- Upserts into `item_prod_order_detail_associations` with `matchSource = 'prepack_code'` or `'item_number'`
- Skips rows already linked (subquery check on association table)
- Returns detailed stats: prepack upserted, direct upserted, missing headers, errors

**Image verification/retry logic** (`getItemIMGData()`):
- Fetches images from Coldlion, writes to local temp files (`./images/`, `./thumbs/`)
- Uploads to GCS, then verifies with HEAD request
- If verification fails or content-length is 0, retries up to 5 times with 1-second delays
- Skips files that cannot be verified; records skipped file count

---

### 8. **Migration-Relevant Observations**

#### **GCP Coupling:**
1. **Cloud Run deployment** (`cloudbuild.yaml`):
   - Service deployed to Google Cloud Run (private, `--no-allow-unauthenticated`)
   - Secrets injected from Google Secret Manager (`--set-secrets`)
   - VPC connector for private database access
   - No hard dependency on GCP SDKs in code, but infrastructure-bound

2. **Cloud SQL assumption**:
   - `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT` point to GCP Cloud SQL instance
   - `config/db.config.js` validates all DB vars present but does not encode Cloud SQL specifics
   - **Postgres is Postgres** — migrate to self-hosted or Supabase without code changes if credentials change

3. **GCS image storage** (HIGH IMPACT):
   - Hardcoded URLs: `https://storage.googleapis.com/designflow-default-bucket/No_Image.jpg`
   - Self-call to `http://localhost:5001/uploadToGCS` assumes service runs as a single container
   - **Post-migration**: Either
     - Keep GCS (Supabase can stay network-accessible to GCS), or
     - Redirect image uploads to DigitalOcean Spaces / Supabase Storage and repoint URLs

#### **What breaks if the PLM DB moves to Supabase:**
1. **Connection string format**: Supabase provides a standard Postgres `postgresql://` URL. `config/db.config.js` extracts `HOST`, `PORT`, `USER`, `PASSWORD`, `DB` from env vars — **no change needed** if Cloud Run secret mapping injects the same vars.
2. **Schema selection**: `process.env.SCHEMA` still works on Supabase Postgres (schemas are first-class in Postgres).
3. **Sequelize ORM**: Fully compatible with Supabase Postgres (no app-specific Postgres extensions used).
4. **Pool config**: May need tuning (Supabase Cloud has connection limits; adjust `pool.max` from 22 to e.g. 5–10 depending on tier).
5. **Network access**: VPC connector no longer needed; Supabase Cloud/self-hosted must be network-reachable from Cloud Run or same network.

#### **Secrets and environment:**
- `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY`: Still needed for request authentication; no change.
- `COLDLION_API_KEY`: Still needed for Coldlion access; no change.
- `DB_*` secrets: Remapped from Cloud SQL instance name to Supabase connection string vars.
- `SCHEMA`: Already env-driven; set to `designflow` or target schema post-migration.

#### **Already integrated with shared Supabase:**
- This repo is **not** the shared-db owner; it reads from and writes to a schema in the PLM database.
- `shared-db/` subdirectory contains migration docs and rehearsal scripts, **not** production schema ownership.
- The shared-db migration docs (`supabase-migration-prep.md`) mention `plm-sync.timer` as a **host-side systemd timer** that invokes this service — timer itself is **not** in this repo; it's managed by Ansible.

#### **Sync direction post-migration:**
- **No change**: This service remains a **pull-only feeder** from Coldlion → Supabase Postgres.
- No bi-directional sync; Supabase is a read source for item-master/backend/tracking services.
- Other services (CRM, PIM, PM-system) may also write to Supabase (separate integrations), but data-syncing does not.

#### **Key migration tasks:**
1. **Dump/restore** production data from Cloud SQL to Supabase (one-time, preserve all tables and constraints).
2. **Update Cloud Build trigger** substitutions or Cloud Run deploy to inject Supabase connection secrets (e.g., `DB_HOST` → Supabase host, `DB_PORT` → 5432, etc.).
3. **Verify schema selection**: Confirm `SCHEMA=designflow` (or target schema) is injected.
4. **Connection pool tuning**: Reduce `pool.max` if Supabase tier limits concurrent connections.
5. **Image storage strategy**: Decide whether to keep GCS or migrate to Supabase Storage / DigitalOcean Spaces. Update hardcoded `https://storage.googleapis.com/` URLs if changing.
6. **Host-side systemd timer** (`plm-sync.timer` in `u2giants/ansible`): No change to timer config, but it should invoke the new Supabase-backed service instance. May need to update Cloud Run service URL if custom domain changes.

---

### **Files to Review During Migration:**

| File | Purpose | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| `config/db.config.js` | DB connection validation | Low — update env var injection only |
| `models/db.js` | Sequelize bootstrap & pool config | Medium — may tune pool for Supabase tier limits |
| `index.js` | API key gate & middleware | None |
| `routes/lib.controller.js` | Sync endpoints | None (stays pull-based from Coldlion) |
| `models/lib.model.js` | Sync logic (find-or-create, upsert, prepack) | None (ORM queries compatible) |
| `cloudbuild.yaml` | Cloud Build secrets injection | Medium — update trigger substitution values for Supabase creds |
| `helpers/utility.js` | Field remapping | Low — values unchanged, just targets Supabase schema |
| `models/db/*.js` | Sequelize model definitions | None (schema names/structure unchanged) |
| `Dockerfile` | Container image | Low — still `node:lts-alpine`, no GCP-specific deps |

---

### **Summary**

`designflow-data-syncing` is a **clean, stateless ETL service** with **no Supabase-specific features** but also **no Supabase awareness yet**. It is **entirely GCP Cloud SQL and Cloud Run agnostic at the application layer** — the coupling is only at the infrastructure/deployment layer (Cloud Build, Secret Manager, VPC connector). **Migration to Supabase requires only environment and secrets updates**; the code itself is portable. The main post-migration decision is **image storage strategy** (GCS vs. Spaces vs. Supabase Storage) and **connection pool tuning** for Supabase tier limits.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive knowledge of the service. Let me compile the structured analysis report.

---

## PLM Data-Syncing Service Analysis: GCP Cloud SQL to Supabase Migration

### 1. **Purpose & Responsibility**

The `designflow-data-syncing` service is a **private system-to-system ETL/integration layer** that acts as the Coldlion → Designflow synchronization boundary. It is **not user-facing**; every request requires `X-API-Key` authentication.

**Data flows:**
- **Source (Coldlion ERP)** → `designflow-data-syncing` → **Destination (shared PLM PostgreSQL schema)**
- Pulls master data from Coldlion API (external inventory/ERP system) via paginated HTTP calls
- Upserts into the shared Designflow database schema (same schema used by `designflow-backend`, `designflow-item-master`, `designflow-tracking`)
- Downstream consumers (item-master, backend, tracking services) read the synced data

**Primary sync domains:**
- **Item master**: item headers, item details, item attachments (images uploaded to GCS)
- **Merch groups**: merchandising hierarchy headers and details
- **Production orders**: order headers and details
- **External references**: vendor and customer records
- **Prepack associations**: production order detail ↔ item mappings

**Responsibility scope** (per `AGENTS.md` and `system-architecture.md`):
- Fetch paginated Coldlion data over HTTPS with `COLDLION_API_KEY`
- Transform/remap Coldlion field names to internal DB columns (`helpers/utility.js`)
- Find-or-create and upsert rows with `modTime`-based freshness guards
- Sync streaming for merch group detail with Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- Bulk upsert production orders and prepack associations

---

### 2. **Tech Stack**

- **Runtime**: Node.js LTS (CommonJS, no TypeScript)
- **Framework**: Express.js
- **ORM**: Sequelize v6 (models-based, no separate migration tool)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL via `pg` v8
- **HTTP client**: Axios v1
- **Server-side job scheduler**: None (API-driven sync, no cron/scheduled jobs in this repo; host-level systemd timers invoke endpoints)
- **Testing**: Jest (unit tests only); Playwright boilerplate (not wired to service)
- **Container**: Docker (`node:lts-alpine`), built via Cloud Build
- **Image processing**: ImageMagick (installed in Docker for image resize/conversion)
- **File upload utility**: Multer for multipart/form-data handling

**Key dependencies** in `package.json`:
```json
"axios": "^1.13.5",
"express": "^4.22.1",
"pg": "^8.11.5",
"sequelize": "^6.37.3",
"dotenv": "^17.3.1",
"jest": "^29.7.0"
```

---

### 3. **Database Access**

**Connection source:** `models/db.js` initializes a **single shared Sequelize instance** to one PostgreSQL database.

**Configuration** (`config/db.config.js`):
- Reads from env: `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`
- Throws if any required var is missing
- No built-in fallback to local/socket connection

**Sequelize pool** (`models/db.js`):
```javascript
pool: {
  max: 22,           // max connections
  min: 1,            // min connections
  acquire: 600000,   // 10-min acquire timeout
  idle: 10000        // 10-sec idle timeout
}
```

**Key dialect options:**
```javascript
dialectOptions: {
  connectTimeout: 300000,  // 5-min initial connect timeout
  client_encoding: 'UTF8'  // Force UTF-8 wire encoding (defense against CP437 mojibake)
}
```

**Schema selection:** `process.env.SCHEMA` (e.g., `designflow_dev`, `designflow`) selected at connect time; Sequelize models all target `sequelize.define(..., { schema: process.env.SCHEMA })`.

**Sync patterns:**
- **Find-or-create**: Uses composite keys (`mgTypeCode + mg_desc + ItemNoCode + divisionCode_fk`, etc.) scoped to division/company
- **Upsert with `modTime` guard**: Bulk inserts with `ignoreDuplicates: true`, then per-row updates only if `modTime` is older (`[Op.lt]`)
- **Bulk operations**: `bulkCreate()` with `ignoreDuplicates`, followed by per-row `update()` loops with `modTime` filtering
- **Transactions**: None explicitly used; each sync function is atomic at the Sequelize level (no explicit `transaction()` wrapper)

**Migrations:**
- No separate migration tool (e.g., Sequelize CLI migrations, Knex, Flyway)
- Startup: `sequelize.sync()` creates missing tables from model definitions
- Column additions: Idempotent inline `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` in `models/db.js` (see `AGENTS.md` §11 for mixed-case identifier quoting requirement)

**Model files** (`models/db/*.js`, physically duplicated from `designflow-backend`):
- `itemHeader.js`, `itemDetail.js`, `itemAttachment.js` — item master tables
- `merchGroup.js`, `merchGroupHeaders.js` — merch hierarchy
- `ProdOrderHeader.js`, `ProdOrderDetail.js` — production orders
- `externalVendor.js`, `externalCustomer.js` — external vendor/customer records (PKs: `vendorCode`, `customerCode`)
- `item_prod_order_detail_associations.js` — prepack-association bridge table

---

### 4. **Sync Flows**

All sync is **pull-based**: the service fetches from Coldlion, not the reverse. Each route is stateless and idempotent (upsert-safe).

| Route | Source | Destination | Frequency | Key table(s) | Dedup/upsert key | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `GET /getItemHeaderFromCL?id=1` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/itemheader?page=N&sort=createdtime,desc` | `itemHeader` | Manual (Saturday check for mod date) | `itemHeader` | Composite: `item_num_id` | Paginated fetch; remaps 50+ Coldlion fields via `utility.remapItemHeader()` |
| `GET /getItemDetailFromCL` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/itemDetails` | `itemDetail` | Manual | `itemDetail` | `item_pk` (Sequelize primary key) | Remaps 40+ Coldlion fields via `utility.remapItemDetail()` |
| `POST /getItem` | — | `itemHeader` + associations | Manual | `itemHeader`, `itemAttachment` | N/A | Returns item info with attachments; no upsert |
| `GET /getMerchgroupDetailFromCL?companyCode=EDGEHOME&mgTypeCodes=01,02,...&divisionCodes=CW001,...` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/merchGroupDetails?companyCode=...&mgTypeCode=...&divisionCode=...` | `merchGroup` | Manual | `merchGroup` | Composite: `mgTypeCode` + `mg_desc` + `mg_code` + `ItemNoCode` + `divisionCode_fk` + `companyCode_fk` | Loops over divisions (CW001, SP001, EH001) and merch types (01–10); remaps via `utility.remapMGDetail()` |
| `POST /streamMerchgroupDetailFromCL` (body: `{companyCode, mgTypeCode, divisionCode}`) | Coldlion `/EhpApi/merchGroupDetails` | `merchGroup` | Manual (SSE streaming) | `merchGroup` | Same composite key | Uses Server-Sent Events for real-time progress feedback; finds-or-creates with `modTime` ordering |
| `GET /getMerchGroupHeaders` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/merchGroupHeaders?companyCode=EDGEHOME&divisionCode=EH001` | `merchGroupHeaders` | Manual | `merchGroupHeaders` | N/A | Fetches headers; find-or-create |
| `GET /getProdOrderHeader` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/prodtracking?createdFrom=<3 days ago>` | `ProdOrderHeader` | Manual (filter: last 3 days) | `ProdOrderHeader` | N/A | `bulkCreate(..., {ignoreDuplicates: true})`; no additional upsert |
| `GET /getProdOrderDetail` | Loops all `ProdOrderHeader.prodOrderNo`, fetches `/EhpApi/proddetails?prodOrderNo=...` | `ProdOrderDetail` | Manual (full scan of existing headers) | `ProdOrderDetail` | `pkey` | Per-row update if stored `modTime < incoming modTime` |
| `GET /getExternalVendor` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/vendors?createdFrom=<today>&size=2000` | `externalVendor` | Manual (last 24h) | `externalVendor` | `vendorCode` (PK) | `bulkCreate()` + per-row `update()` with `modTime` guard |
| `GET /getExternalCustomer` | Coldlion `/EhpApi/customers?createdFrom=<today>&size=2000` | `externalCustomer` | Manual (last 24h) | `externalCustomer` | `customerCode` (PK) | Same pattern as vendors |
| `GET /syncPrepackAssociations` | Reads all `ProdOrderDetail` where `prepackCode != null`, calls `/EhpApi/prepackDetail?prepackCode=...`; also reads direct items | Coldlion + bridge table `item_prod_order_detail_associations` | Manual | `item_prod_order_detail_associations`, `itemHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` | Composite: `item_header_id` + `prod_order_detail_pkey` + `match_source` | For each prepack code, fetches subitems and links to matched `itemHeader` rows; skips rows already processed (subqueries to check if already in association table) |

**Notable patterns:**
- **Company code mapping** (hardcoded in `utility.js`): `EDGEHOME → 1`, else `→ 2`
- **Division code mapping** (hardcoded in `utility.js`): `CW001 → 1`, `SP001 → 8`, `EH001 → 9`
- **`modTime` freshness**: All upserts check incoming `modTime` against stored `modTime`; only updates if incoming is **newer** (`Op.lt` condition)
- **Pagination**: Item headers paginated at source; merch group detail not paginated (assumes size ≤ API limit)
- **Error handling**: Widely swallowed (axios `.catch()` blocks just `console.log()` and return `undefined`); callers proceed with empty data (see `AGENTS.md` §11)

---

### 5. **Auth**

**Inbound (request auth):**
- All routes protected by `X-API-Key: <DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY>` middleware in `index.js`
- Health check also requires the key (no exception)
- Invalid key → HTTP 500 "not valid key"
- Key stored in `process.env.DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY` (injected from Secret Manager in Cloud Run)

**Outbound (Coldlion calls):**
- Every axios request to Coldlion includes `headers: { "X-API-Key": process.env.COLDLION_API_KEY }`
- Coldlion API base: `http://x5.coldlion.com/EhpApi/...` (hardcoded in most routes, fetched from `externalApi` table in one case)
- Key stored in `process.env.COLDLION_API_KEY` (injected from Secret Manager)

**Service-to-service (in deployment):**
- BFF invokes data-syncing via Cloud Run OIDC token + API key
- No inter-service user-identity propagation (this is a system service, not user-facing)

---

### 6. **External Integrations**

| System | Endpoint | Protocol | Purpose | Credentials |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Coldlion** | `http://x5.coldlion.com/EhpApi/*` | HTTPS (Axios) | Pull item, merch group, prod order, vendor, customer, prepack data | `COLDLION_API_KEY` header |
| **GCS (Google Cloud Storage)** | `https://storage.googleapis.com/` | HTTPS | Upload item images/thumbnails (from sync) | Service account (implicit in Cloud Run) |
| **DigitalOcean Spaces** | N/A (not used in this service) | — | — | — |
| **Directus** | N/A (shared DB is Postgres, not Directus API) | — | — | — |

**Image handling (GCS coupling):**
- Item image sync (`/getItemHeaderFromCL?id=5`) uploads images to `gs://designflow-default-bucket/` via a self-call to `http://localhost:5001/uploadToGCS`
- Images stored locally in `./images/` and `./thumbs/` directories, then uploaded to GCS
- GCS URLs hardcoded to `https://storage.googleapis.com/designflow-default-bucket/` (later rewritten to `https://storage.cloud.google.com/`)
- No fallback storage; if GCS upload fails, a default "No_Image.jpg" is used
- Default image: `https://storage.googleapis.com/designflow-default-bucket/No_Image.jpg`

---

### 7. **Heavy Business Logic**

**Transformation/remapping** (`helpers/utility.js`):
- `remapItemHeader()`: Maps 50+ Coldlion item fields → internal item columns (e.g., `itemDesc` → `item_descr_name`, `itemCost` → `item_cost_ext`). Includes hardcoded company/division code mappings.
- `remapMGDetail()`: Maps merch group fields → internal columns; includes hardcoded division → `divisionCode_id_fk` map.
- `remapItemDetail()`: Maps 40+ item detail fields (UPC, EAN, GTIN, sizes, prices, UDFs, etc.)
- **All remapping includes field-level transformations** (e.g., boolean flags, code→ID mappings, null defaults for unimplemented fields).

**Deduplication/reconciliation** (`models/lib.model.js`):
- **Find-or-create by composite key**: For merch groups, checks all of `mgTypeCode`, `mg_desc`, `mg_code`, `ItemNoCode`, `divisionCode_fk`, `companyCode_fk` before deciding insert vs. update.
- **Freshness guard (`modTime`)**: Every update only proceeds if incoming `modTime > stored modTime`. Uses SQL `WHERE modTime < incoming` to prevent stale overwrites.
- **Bulk insert + per-row update pattern**: For performance, `bulkCreate(…, {ignoreDuplicates: true})` inserts new rows in one batch, then loops through all rows to update existing ones if fresher. Not a single upsert operation.

**Prepack association logic** (`Customer.syncPrepackAssociations()`):
- Groups `ProdOrderDetail` rows by `prepackCode`
- For each prepack, fetches `/EhpApi/prepackDetail?prepackCode=...` to get sub-items
- For each sub-item, finds matching `itemHeader` by `item_num_id` (normalized, cached to avoid repeated lookups)
- Upserts into `item_prod_order_detail_associations` with `matchSource = 'prepack_code'` or `'item_number'`
- Skips rows already linked (subquery check on association table)
- Returns detailed stats: prepack upserted, direct upserted, missing headers, errors

**Image verification/retry logic** (`getItemIMGData()`):
- Fetches images from Coldlion, writes to local temp files (`./images/`, `./thumbs/`)
- Uploads to GCS, then verifies with HEAD request
- If verification fails or content-length is 0, retries up to 5 times with 1-second delays
- Skips files that cannot be verified; records skipped file count

---

### 8. **Migration-Relevant Observations**

#### **GCP Coupling:**
1. **Cloud Run deployment** (`cloudbuild.yaml`):
   - Service deployed to Google Cloud Run (private, `--no-allow-unauthenticated`)
   - Secrets injected from Google Secret Manager (`--set-secrets`)
   - VPC connector for private database access
   - No hard dependency on GCP SDKs in code, but infrastructure-bound

2. **Cloud SQL assumption**:
   - `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT` point to GCP Cloud SQL instance
   - `config/db.config.js` validates all DB vars present but does not encode Cloud SQL specifics
   - **Postgres is Postgres** — migrate to self-hosted or Supabase without code changes if credentials change

3. **GCS image storage** (HIGH IMPACT):
   - Hardcoded URLs: `https://storage.googleapis.com/designflow-default-bucket/No_Image.jpg`
   - Self-call to `http://localhost:5001/uploadToGCS` assumes service runs as a single container
   - **Post-migration**: Either
     - Keep GCS (Supabase can stay network-accessible to GCS), or
     - Redirect image uploads to DigitalOcean Spaces / Supabase Storage and repoint URLs

#### **What breaks if the PLM DB moves to Supabase:**
1. **Connection string format**: Supabase provides a standard Postgres `postgresql://` URL. `config/db.config.js` extracts `HOST`, `PORT`, `USER`, `PASSWORD`, `DB` from env vars — **no change needed** if Cloud Run secret mapping injects the same vars.
2. **Schema selection**: `process.env.SCHEMA` still works on Supabase Postgres (schemas are first-class in Postgres).
3. **Sequelize ORM**: Fully compatible with Supabase Postgres (no app-specific Postgres extensions used).
4. **Pool config**: May need tuning (Supabase Cloud has connection limits; adjust `pool.max` from 22 to e.g. 5–10 depending on tier).
5. **Network access**: VPC connector no longer needed; Supabase Cloud/self-hosted must be network-reachable from Cloud Run or same network.

#### **Secrets and environment:**
- `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY`: Still needed for request authentication; no change.
- `COLDLION_API_KEY`: Still needed for Coldlion access; no change.
- `DB_*` secrets: Remapped from Cloud SQL instance name to Supabase connection string vars.
- `SCHEMA`: Already env-driven; set to `designflow` or target schema post-migration.

#### **Already integrated with shared Supabase:**
- This repo is **not** the shared-db owner; it reads from and writes to a schema in the PLM database.
- `shared-db/` subdirectory contains migration docs and rehearsal scripts, **not** production schema ownership.
- The shared-db migration docs (`supabase-migration-prep.md`) mention `plm-sync.timer` as a **host-side systemd timer** that invokes this service — timer itself is **not** in this repo; it's managed by Ansible.

#### **Sync direction post-migration:**
- **No change**: This service remains a **pull-only feeder** from Coldlion → Supabase Postgres.
- No bi-directional sync; Supabase is a read source for item-master/backend/tracking services.
- Other services (CRM, PIM, PM-system) may also write to Supabase (separate integrations), but data-syncing does not.

#### **Key migration tasks:**
1. **Dump/restore** production data from Cloud SQL to Supabase (one-time, preserve all tables and constraints).
2. **Update Cloud Build trigger** substitutions or Cloud Run deploy to inject Supabase connection secrets (e.g., `DB_HOST` → Supabase host, `DB_PORT` → 5432, etc.).
3. **Verify schema selection**: Confirm `SCHEMA=designflow` (or target schema) is injected.
4. **Connection pool tuning**: Reduce `pool.max` if Supabase tier limits concurrent connections.
5. **Image storage strategy**: Decide whether to keep GCS or migrate to Supabase Storage / DigitalOcean Spaces. Update hardcoded `https://storage.googleapis.com/` URLs if changing.
6. **Host-side systemd timer** (`plm-sync.timer` in `u2giants/ansible`): No change to timer config, but it should invoke the new Supabase-backed service instance. May need to update Cloud Run service URL if custom domain changes.

---

### **Files to Review During Migration:**

| File | Purpose | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| `config/db.config.js` | DB connection validation | Low — update env var injection only |
| `models/db.js` | Sequelize bootstrap & pool config | Medium — may tune pool for Supabase tier limits |
| `index.js` | API key gate & middleware | None |
| `routes/lib.controller.js` | Sync endpoints | None (stays pull-based from Coldlion) |
| `models/lib.model.js` | Sync logic (find-or-create, upsert, prepack) | None (ORM queries compatible) |
| `cloudbuild.yaml` | Cloud Build secrets injection | Medium — update trigger substitution values for Supabase creds |
| `helpers/utility.js` | Field remapping | Low — values unchanged, just targets Supabase schema |
| `models/db/*.js` | Sequelize model definitions | None (schema names/structure unchanged) |
| `Dockerfile` | Container image | Low — still `node:lts-alpine`, no GCP-specific deps |

---

### **Summary**

`designflow-data-syncing` is a **clean, stateless ETL service** with **no Supabase-specific features** but also **no Supabase awareness yet**. It is **entirely GCP Cloud SQL and Cloud Run agnostic at the application layer** — the coupling is only at the infrastructure/deployment layer (Cloud Build, Secret Manager, VPC connector). **Migration to Supabase requires only environment and secrets updates**; the code itself is portable. The main post-migration decision is **image storage strategy** (GCS vs. Spaces vs. Supabase Storage) and **connection pool tuning** for Supabase tier limits.
