## Designflow Frontend: Supabase Backend Migration Analysis

### 1. Purpose & Responsibility

**Framework & Role:** Angular 21.1.3 single-page application (SPA) serving the Designflow PLM platform. This is the browser UI layer only — no server-side rendering, no backend ownership. Responsible for all UI assembly, client-side routing, business-logic calculations (margin chains, landed-cost formulas), and backend API orchestration.

**Feature Areas:**
- **RFQ (Request for Quote):** Primary grid-based workflow for vendor quoting, margin/sell-price calculations across 4 INCOTERM bases (FOB, MDDP, POE, WHSE)
- **Item Library:** Product master data management with server-side row model pagination, filtering, sorting
- **Item Detail:** Single-item document view (from `designflow-item-master`)
- **Art Piece Management:** Art/image asset tracking
- **Standardized Products:** Template library for RFQ item prototyping
- **Production Tracking:** Factory production stage tracking with master-detail grids
- **Licensing Tracking:** Royalty and licensing item workflows
- **Sample Tracking:** Physical sample movement tracking (factories ↔ offices, customer shipments) via event timeline
- **Admin / Editor:** Role-restricted reference data configuration (AI models, tariffs, factory country map, HTS rate overrides, Trump 2025 tariff tables)
- **Notifications:** In-app bell badge and notification center
- **User Management:** Admin impersonation, profile management
- **Comments & Attachments:** Per-item notes and file uploads

Located at: `C:/repos/.dflow-audit/designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/`

---

### 2. Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| **Framework** | Angular 21.1.3 with Angular Material 21.1.3, Angular CDK 21.1.3 |
| **Data Grid** | AG Grid Enterprise 35.3.0 (client-side row model for RFQ; server-side row model for Item Library, Production/Licensing Tracking) |
| **HTTP Client** | `HttpClient` (Angular built-in); no axios, no fetch wrapper |
| **Async** | RxJS 7.8.1 — all async via Observables; `firstValueFrom()` enforced (no `.toPromise()`) |
| **Supplementary UI** | PrimeNG 19.1.4, Tailwind CSS 2.0.4 |
| **Auth** | `@azure/msal-browser` 5.11.0 (Azure AD SSO popup flow) + `@auth0/angular-jwt` 5.0.2 (JWT decode, token helpers) |
| **State Management** | None (no Redux, no NgRx) — RxJS Observables + RxJS `shareReplay()` for reference-data caching |
| **CSS** | Tailwind CSS 2.0.4; also uses Vex layout framework (committed into `src/@vex/`) |
| **Build & Serve** | Angular CLI; Docker + nginx-brotli for production serving (Cloud Run) |
| **Tests** | Jest 30.0.0 (unit), Playwright 1.60.0 (e2e); `jest-preset-angular` for Angular support |

Full dependencies: `package.json` at C:/repos/.dflow-audit/designflow-frontend/package.json

---

### 3. Backend Access Pattern

**Architecture:** Frontend → BFF → {designflow-backend, designflow-item-master, designflow-tracking, designflow-data-syncing}

The frontend does NOT call backend services directly. All traffic routes through a BFF (backend-for-frontend) proxy that:
- Accepts the user's Designflow JWT in `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
- Forwards it as `X-User-Authorization` to backend services
- Injects its own Google Cloud Run OIDC token for service-to-service auth

**Environment-Based URLs** (baked into bundle at build time; no runtime injection):

| Environment | Core | Item Master | Tracking | Data Sync |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `development` (localhost) | `http://localhost:5000/api/core` | `http://localhost:5003/api/item_master` | `http://localhost:5002/api/tracking` | `http://localhost:5001/api/data_sync` |
| `staging` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |
| `sandbox` (albert) | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |
| `production` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |

Files: `src/environments/environment.{ts,prod,staging,sandbox}.ts`

**Primary API Service Consumers:**

| Service | Base URL | Purpose |
|---------|----------|---------|
| `main.service.ts` (569 lines) | `environment.url` | **Core RFQ, vendors, grid layout, comments, notifications, notifications, auth (~120 methods)** |
| `std.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Standardized products |
| `admin.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Admin reference data (art types, artist types) |
| `ai-settings.service.ts` | `environment.url` | AI model settings, tariff uploads (Section 301), factory country map |
| `cross.service.ts` | `environment.url` | CBP CROSS proxy (HTS code lookup) |
| `customer.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Customer CRUD + logo upload |
| `item_library.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + '/lib'` | Item Library grid data, item types, item creation |
| `item_detail.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + '/detail'` | Single-item document |
| `art_piece.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + '/artPiece'` | Art piece CRUD |
| `tracking.service.ts` | `environment.tracking` | Production/Licensing/Sample tracking grids + detail updates |
| `licensing_tracking.service.ts` | `environment.tracking + '/lic'` | Licensing-specific (royalty calculations, images) |
| `sync.service.ts` | `environment.data_sync` | Data sync (merch-group sync, coldlion streams) |
| `lead-time.service.ts` | `environment.tracking + '/lead-time'` | Lead time calculator |

**Token Attachment:**
- All services manually call `this.setUserAuth()` to build headers with `Authorization: Bearer <token>` from `localStorage.getItem('token')`
- Alternative: `this.setUserAuthWithLoader()` adds `X-Enable-Loader: true` to trigger the global spinner
- **No automatic token refresh** — tokens expire, user must re-login

**No direct Supabase usage today:**
- No `@supabase/supabase-js` dependency in `package.json`
- No Supabase client code in the frontend
- All backend calls today route through the BFF

Grep result: zero matches for "supabase" across the codebase.

---

### 4. Auth

**Login Paths:**

1. **Password Login:**
   - User submits email + password via login form
   - `POST /auth/login` → backend verifies, returns Designflow RS256 JWT
   - Frontend stores in `localStorage` under key `'token'`

2. **Microsoft Azure AD SSO:**
   - `AzureSsoService` (thin wrapper around `@azure/msal-browser` 5.11.0)
   - User clicks "Sign in with Microsoft" → MSAL popup opens
   - Microsoft login → popup redirects to `/assets/auth-redirect.html` (MSAL redirect bridge)
   - Bridge fires `BroadcastChannel` message with Azure ID token back to main window
   - Frontend exchanges Azure ID token → `POST /auth/azure` → backend verifies via Azure JWKS, returns Designflow JWT
   - Frontend stores returned token in `localStorage`

**Token Storage:**
- `localStorage` (NOT httpOnly; reliant on Angular template sanitization + strict CSP for XSS mitigation)
- Cleared on logout via `localStorage.clear()`
- Survives page refresh
- **MSAL redirect bridge at `/assets/auth-redirect.html`** loads `msal-redirect-bridge.min.js` (copied from `node_modules/@azure/msal-browser/lib/redirect-bridge` at build time)

**Token Injection:**
- `UserAuthInterceptor` (`src/app/helpers/interceptor/user-auth.interceptor.ts`) runs first in the HTTP interceptor chain
- Checks `localStorage.getItem('token')`, clones the request, and sets `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
- No token refresh — expired tokens simply fail requests, HTTP error interceptor shows a snackbar

**Role & Authorization:**
- JWT payload contains `role` (string: `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor`)
- Route guards:
  - `AuthGuardService` — verifies token is present and valid (calls backend + checks expiry)
  - `RoleGuardService` — decodes JWT via `jwt_decode`, reads `route.data.role`, enforces match
- Navigation menu in `app.component.ts` applies role-based `hidden` flags per sidenav item

**Impersonation (Admin-only):**
- Admin clicks "Impersonate" on a user row → `ImpersonationService.startImpersonation(userId)`
- Calls `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId` → backend returns a 2-hour RS256 JWT signed as the target user + `impersonated_by` claim
- Frontend saves admin's current token to `admin_token` in `localStorage`, replaces `token` with the impersonation token
- `UserAuthInterceptor` picks up the new token automatically
- Orange "Viewing as **[Name]** — Stop Impersonating" banner shown at top of page
- `stopImpersonation()` restores `admin_token` → `token`, uses hard `window.location.href = '/'` to reload and clear in-memory state

---

### 5. Heavy Client-Side Logic

**RFQ Margin & Sell-Price Calculations** (business-critical):
- **File:** `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/equation.ts` (large formula library)
- **Master grid:** 4518 lines in `rfq.component.ts` managing 100+ columns, valueSetters, and the calculation cascade
- **Formulas:** All margin/sell-price/landed-cost math lives client-side; persisted to backend only on `onSave`
- **Valued setters:** When user edits FOB Cost, CBM/pc, agent commission, duty rate, freight, or container cost:
  1. `rfq.component.ts` valueSetter calls `calculateLandedCost()` → updates `landed_cost` field
  2. Calls `rfqEquation.recalculate_Lic_Gen(params, colHeaders)`
  3. This chains 8 setter methods: `setGen{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()` and `setLic{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()`
  4. Each setter computes sell price from margin, or margin from sell price, using cost helper functions
- **Async valueSetters:** Return Promise; rejection cancels edit and reverts cell
- **Cost Helpers:** `getFobTotalCost()`, `getMddpTotalCost()`, `getPoeTotalCost()`, `getWhseTotalCost()` — apply duty rate, freight, CBM, warehouse cost based on INCOTERM
- **Dependency:** Grid column identifiers use both `field` (human-readable, can have spaces: `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`) and `colId` (stable identifier: `"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`). The master `getMasterFiledName(colId)` lookup function maps colId → field. This coupling is tight and unavoidable (every calculation must look up column names by ID).

**AG Grid Master-Detail Grids** (RFQ, Production Tracking):
- Master row = RFQ Item or Production Order
- Detail row = vendor quotes or production sub-items
- Detail grid loaded dynamically on row expansion via `DetailCellRenderer` component
- Detail grid has its own column definitions fetched from backend (`getRfqDetailGridHeader()` for RFQ)
- This N+1 expansion caching was fixed via `shareReplay()` in `main.service.ts` (added 2026-06-22)
- **Breaking change risk:** If direct-Supabase client-side access becomes needed, async loading of detail-grid columns could conflict with real-time subscriptions or require schema changes

**Server-Side Row Model (SSRM) Grids** (Item Library, Production Tracking, Licensing Tracking):
- Backend handles pagination (`startRow`, `endRow`), filtering (`filterModel` as JSON), sorting
- Frontend sends `{ startRow, endRow, filterModel }` to backend
- Backend returns rows + total count
- Grid maintains viewport-only data; no N+1 per-row expansion
- **Implication:** These grids are already structured for pagination, but RFQ grid uses client-side row model and loads ALL rows. Server-side migration to the RFQ would be a *separate* large refactor (HANDOFF.md §A).

**Duty Rate & AI Classification**:
- Dialog in `src/app/pages/rfq/duty_rate_dialog/`
- User enters product description → cleaned via `desc-cleaner.ts` (strips brand/size/color)
- Calls `POST /api/openrouter/chat` → AI returns HTS code (no rate calculation)
- Three-step rate lookup:
  1. `GET /api/admin/hts-rates/lookup?hts=` → bundled USITC HTS JSON (10→8→6 digit fallback)
  2. `GET /api/admin/section301/lookup?hts=` → admin overrides first, then parsed USTR CSV/PDF
  3. `GET /api/admin/trump2025/lookup?country={fobCountry}` → per-country tariff table
- Rate stored as **both equation and evaluated total:**
  - DB: `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (e.g., `"5.3+25+10"`)
  - Row: `[duty_rate_field]_equation` (ephemeral, for `mathExprCellEditor`)
  - Cell displays: `"40.3%"` (evaluated)
  - Edit restores: `"5.3+25+10"` (equation)

**Reference-Data Caching:**
- `main.service.ts` has a `refCache` Map that memoizes static reference lists
- `getCached(key, factory)` wraps getters; error eviction prevents stale-data poisoning
- Calls: `getAllVendors()`, `getCustomers()`, `getLicenseList()`, `getDeliveryLocation()`, `getRFQItemStatus()`, `getCompanyCode()`, `getRFQGroup()`, `getRFQStep()`, `getRfqDetailGridHeader()`, `getCBM()`, `getFOBCountry()`
- **Impact:** Direct Supabase migration would need to replicate this caching strategy (or use Supabase realtime subscriptions)

**File Upload** (DigitalOcean Spaces, presigned URLs):
1. Frontend calls `POST /getPreSignedUrl { fileName, contentType }` → backend returns signed PUT URL (15 min expiry)
2. Browser PUTs file directly to Spaces with headers (bypasses HTTP interceptors via raw `fetch`)
3. Frontend calls `POST /setObjectPublic { fileName }` → backend sets ACL to `public-read`
4. Returns `{ publicUrl }` from `DO_PUBLIC_URL` env var
5. URL saved to DB
- **Implication:** This presigned-URL flow is tightly coupled to backend object-storage logic; moving to Supabase Storage would require re-pointing these endpoints.

---

### 6. Migration-Relevant Observations

**Tight Backend Coupling:**

1. **Environment-Based URL Hardcoding**
   - API base URLs are baked into the Angular bundle at build time (`environment.*.ts`)
   - No runtime env injection
   - Changing API targets requires a full rebuild
   - **For Supabase:** Would need to update 4 environment files + rebuild per environment

2. **Service Architecture (Monolithic)**
   - 18 frontend services each call a specific backend service
   - `main.service.ts` is the largest (569 lines, ~120 methods) and owns ~70% of RFQ/core/admin traffic
   - No API abstraction layer — services directly inject `HttpClient` and construct URLs
   - Each service manually attaches auth headers
   - **For Supabase:** Would need to either:
     - Create a `SupabaseClientService` wrapper and wire it into each feature service, OR
     - Replace HTTP calls with Supabase client library calls (e.g., `this.supabase.from('rfq_items').select(...)`)

3. **Endpoint Shape Expectations**
   - RFQ calculations depend on specific backend JSON shapes:
     - `duty_rate` may arrive as `{ title: number }` or raw number — code defensively checks both
     - Vendor quote arrays use capital `Vendors` (not `vendors`)
     - `req_status` integer values (`null`, `1`, `2`) encode quote state machine
     - Presigned URL flow expects endpoint `/getPreSignedUrl` + `/setObjectPublic`
   - **For Supabase:** Direct REST API calls would match Supabase's table schema exactly; no transformation layer, so schema changes break the client

4. **Master-Detail Grid Coupling**
   - RFQ detail grid columns are fetched per expansion: `POST /getRFQViewlayout { grid_id: 'rfq_detail' }`
   - If Supabase realtime subscriptions are used, column metadata would need its own Supabase table + subscription lifecycle
   - Current caching with `shareReplay()` assumes bounded, infrequent fetches; realtime could create unexpected socket overhead

5. **Reference Data Cache Strategy**
   - `refCache` in `main.service.ts` assumes:
     - Lists are static or rarely change during a session
     - Mutations call `clearRefCache(key)` to invalidate
     - Error failures evict the entry (retry on next read)
   - **For Supabase realtime:** Would need to subscribe to reference tables + keep cache hot, replacing the per-request memoization model

6. **No Direct Supabase Dependency**
   - `package.json` has no `@supabase/supabase-js`
   - All HTTP calls use Angular `HttpClient`
   - Switching to Supabase would require:
     - Adding `@supabase/supabase-js` to dependencies
     - Rewriting service methods from `this.http.get/post/patch` to `this.supabase.from().select()`
     - Handling Supabase RLS (row-level security) policies — JWT must contain correct claims
     - Testing realtime subscriptions + unsubscribe cleanup in component `ngOnDestroy`

7. **HTTP Interceptor Stack**
   - `UserAuthInterceptor` (first) — adds `Authorization` header
   - `LoaderInterceptor` (second) — respects `X-Enable-Loader` header
   - `HttpErrorInterceptor` (third) — catches errors, shows snackbar
   - **For Supabase:** Realtime subscriptions do not flow through HTTP interceptors; auth must be attached to the client instance instead

8. **Large RFQ Component Complexity** (4518 lines)
   - Master grid initialization: 
     - Fetch column headers (`getGridHeader()`)
     - Fetch row data (`getAllItem()`)
     - Restore saved view layout (`getCustomGridLayout()`)
     - Three operations are parallelized via `forkJoin` (added 2026-06-22)
   - **For Supabase:** Would need to fetch from Supabase tables + subscriptions (columns table, items table, layout table) — potentially three separate `supabase.from()` calls

9. **Deferred Performance Work** (HANDOFF.md §A)
   - RFQ grid's backend `getAllItem` returns unbounded rows (~3000+)
   - **Breaking change risk:** If migrating to Supabase, must pair with SSRM (server-side row model) migration
   - Current client-side row model loses filtering/sorting across full dataset if pagination is added without SSRM

10. **Shared DB / Supabase Migration Context**
    - This repo includes `shared-db/` (synced from `u2giants/shared-db`)
    - Shared DB documents unified schema for DAM, CRM, PM, and PLM on Supabase
    - Designflow PLM tables are documented in `shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md`
    - Migrations are staged in `shared-db/supabase/migrations/` (rehearsal-only; not yet applied to live Supabase)
    - **Status:** Schema design is **underway**, but Designflow frontend has NOT yet been updated to use Supabase

---

## Summary

The Designflow frontend is a tightly-coupled, calculation-heavy Angular SPA that orchestrates RFQ pricing, inventory, and tracking workflows via a multi-service REST API. **It has zero direct Supabase dependency today.** A GCP Cloud SQL → Supabase migration would require:

1. **Schema alignment:** Ensure Supabase tables match the JSON shapes the frontend expects (especially RFQ, vendor quote, duty-rate structures)
2. **Service rewrites:** Either wrap Supabase client into each existing service, or rewrite ~120 methods from `HttpClient` to `@supabase/supabase-js`
3. **Auth attachment:** Supabase client needs the same JWT; ensure RLS policies trust the JWT claims (`user_id`, `role`)
4. **Reference data caching:** Replicate `refCache` logic or use Supabase realtime subscriptions (with careful unsubscribe cleanup)
5. **RFQ grid pagination:** Pair any Supabase backend load-balancing with SSRM migration (not implemented yet)
6. **File storage:** Retarget presigned-URL flow to Supabase Storage (if using that) or keep DigitalOcean Spaces
7. **HTTP interceptor assumptions:** Realtime subscriptions bypass interceptors; auth must be baked into Supabase client config

The frontend itself is **low-risk for the migration** (it's just a consumer of APIs); the backend contract changes and schema shape dependencies are the critical unknowns.

## Designflow Frontend: Supabase Backend Migration Analysis

### 1. Purpose & Responsibility

**Framework & Role:** Angular 21.1.3 single-page application (SPA) serving the Designflow PLM platform. This is the browser UI layer only — no server-side rendering, no backend ownership. Responsible for all UI assembly, client-side routing, business-logic calculations (margin chains, landed-cost formulas), and backend API orchestration.

**Feature Areas:**
- **RFQ (Request for Quote):** Primary grid-based workflow for vendor quoting, margin/sell-price calculations across 4 INCOTERM bases (FOB, MDDP, POE, WHSE)
- **Item Library:** Product master data management with server-side row model pagination, filtering, sorting
- **Item Detail:** Single-item document view (from `designflow-item-master`)
- **Art Piece Management:** Art/image asset tracking
- **Standardized Products:** Template library for RFQ item prototyping
- **Production Tracking:** Factory production stage tracking with master-detail grids
- **Licensing Tracking:** Royalty and licensing item workflows
- **Sample Tracking:** Physical sample movement tracking (factories ↔ offices, customer shipments) via event timeline
- **Admin / Editor:** Role-restricted reference data configuration (AI models, tariffs, factory country map, HTS rate overrides, Trump 2025 tariff tables)
- **Notifications:** In-app bell badge and notification center
- **User Management:** Admin impersonation, profile management
- **Comments & Attachments:** Per-item notes and file uploads

Located at: `C:/repos/.dflow-audit/designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/`

---

### 2. Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| **Framework** | Angular 21.1.3 with Angular Material 21.1.3, Angular CDK 21.1.3 |
| **Data Grid** | AG Grid Enterprise 35.3.0 (client-side row model for RFQ; server-side row model for Item Library, Production/Licensing Tracking) |
| **HTTP Client** | `HttpClient` (Angular built-in); no axios, no fetch wrapper |
| **Async** | RxJS 7.8.1 — all async via Observables; `firstValueFrom()` enforced (no `.toPromise()`) |
| **Supplementary UI** | PrimeNG 19.1.4, Tailwind CSS 2.0.4 |
| **Auth** | `@azure/msal-browser` 5.11.0 (Azure AD SSO popup flow) + `@auth0/angular-jwt` 5.0.2 (JWT decode, token helpers) |
| **State Management** | None (no Redux, no NgRx) — RxJS Observables + RxJS `shareReplay()` for reference-data caching |
| **CSS** | Tailwind CSS 2.0.4; also uses Vex layout framework (committed into `src/@vex/`) |
| **Build & Serve** | Angular CLI; Docker + nginx-brotli for production serving (Cloud Run) |
| **Tests** | Jest 30.0.0 (unit), Playwright 1.60.0 (e2e); `jest-preset-angular` for Angular support |

Full dependencies: `package.json` at C:/repos/.dflow-audit/designflow-frontend/package.json

### 3. Backend Access Pattern

**Architecture:** Frontend → BFF → {designflow-backend, designflow-item-master, designflow-tracking, designflow-data-syncing}

The frontend does NOT call backend services directly. All traffic routes through a BFF (backend-for-frontend) proxy that:
- Accepts the user's Designflow JWT in `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
- Forwards it as `X-User-Authorization` to backend services
- Injects its own Google Cloud Run OIDC token for service-to-service auth

- **Formulas:** All margin/sell-price/landed-cost math lives client-side; persisted to backend only on `onSave`
- **Valued setters:** When user edits FOB Cost, CBM/pc, agent commission, duty rate, freight, or container cost:
  1. `rfq.component.ts` valueSetter calls `calculateLandedCost()` → updates `landed_cost` field
  2. Calls `rfqEquation.recalculate_Lic_Gen(params, colHeaders)`
  3. This chains 8 setter methods: `setGen{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()` and `setLic{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()`
  4. Each setter computes sell price from margin, or margin from sell price, using cost helper functions
- **Async valueSetters:** Return Promise; rejection cancels edit and reverts cell
- **Cost Helpers:** `getFobTotalCost()`, `getMddpTotalCost()`, `getPoeTotalCost()`, `getWhseTotalCost()` — apply duty rate, freight, CBM, warehouse cost based on INCOTERM
- **Dependency:** Grid column identifiers use both `field` (human-readable, can have spaces: `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`) and `colId` (stable identifier: `"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`). The master `getMasterFiledName(colId)` lookup function maps colId → field. This coupling is tight and unavoidable (every calculation must look up column names by ID).

**AG Grid Master-Detail Grids** (RFQ, Production Tracking):
- Master row = RFQ Item or Production Order
- Detail row = vendor quotes or production sub-items
- Detail grid loaded dynamically on row expansion via `DetailCellRenderer` component
- Detail grid has its own column definitions fetched from backend (`getRfqDetailGridHeader()` for RFQ)
- This N+1 expansion caching was fixed via `shareReplay()` in `main.service.ts` (added 2026-06-22)
- **Breaking change risk:** If direct-Supabase client-side access becomes needed, async loading of detail-grid columns could conflict with real-time subscriptions or require schema changes

**Server-Side Row Model (SSRM) Grids** (Item Library, Production Tracking, Licensing Tracking):
- Backend handles pagination (`startRow`, `endRow`), filtering (`filterModel` as JSON), sorting
- Frontend sends `{ startRow, endRow, filterModel }` to backend
- Backend returns rows + total count
- Grid maintains viewport-only data; no N+1 per-row expansion
- **Implication:** These grids are already structured for pagination, but RFQ grid uses client-side row model and loads ALL rows. Server-side migration to the RFQ would be a *separate* large refactor (HANDOFF.md §A).

**Duty Rate & AI Classification**:
- Dialog in `src/app/pages/rfq/duty_rate_dialog/`
- User enters product description → cleaned via `desc-cleaner.ts` (strips brand/size/color)
- Calls `POST /api/openrouter/chat` → AI returns HTS code (no rate calculation)
- Three-step rate lookup:
  1. `GET /api/admin/hts-rates/lookup?hts=` → bundled USITC HTS JSON (10→8→6 digit fallback)
  2. `GET /api/admin/section301/lookup?hts=` → admin overrides first, then parsed USTR CSV/PDF
  3. `GET /api/admin/trump2025/lookup?country={fobCountry}` → per-country tariff table
- Rate stored as **both equation and evaluated total:**
  - DB: `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (e.g., `"5.3+25+10"`)
  - Row: `[duty_rate_field]_equation` (ephemeral, for `mathExprCellEditor`)
  - Cell displays: `"40.3%"` (evaluated)
  - Edit restores: `"5.3+25+10"` (equation)

**Reference-Data Caching:**
- `main.service.ts` has a `refCache` Map that memoizes static reference lists
- `getCached(key, factory)` wraps getters; error eviction prevents stale-data poisoning
- Calls: `getAllVendors()`, `getCustomers()`, `getLicenseList()`, `getDeliveryLocation()`, `getRFQItemStatus()`, `getCompanyCode()`, `getRFQGroup()`, `getRFQStep()`, `getRfqDetailGridHeader()`, `getCBM()`, `getFOBCountry()`
- **Impact:** Direct Supabase migration would need to replicate this caching strategy (or use Supabase realtime subscriptions)

**File Upload** (DigitalOcean Spaces, presigned URLs):
1. Frontend calls `POST /getPreSignedUrl { fileName, contentType }` → backend returns signed PUT URL (15 min expiry)
2. Browser PUTs file directly to Spaces with headers (bypasses HTTP interceptors via raw `fetch`)
3. Frontend calls `POST /setObjectPublic { fileName }` → backend sets ACL to `public-read`
4. Returns `{ publicUrl }` from `DO_PUBLIC_URL` env var
5. URL saved to DB
- **Implication:** This presigned-URL flow is tightly coupled to backend object-storage logic; moving to Supabase Storage would require re-pointing these endpoints.

---

### 6. Migration-Relevant Observations

**Tight Backend Coupling:**

1. **Environment-Based URL Hardcoding**
   - API base URLs are baked into the Angular bundle at build time (`environment.*.ts`)
   - No runtime env injection
   - Changing API targets requires a full rebuild
   - **For Supabase:** Would need to update 4 environment files + rebuild per environment

2. **Service Architecture (Monolithic)**
   - 18 frontend services each call a specific backend service
   - `main.service.ts` is the largest (569 lines, ~120 methods) and owns ~70% of RFQ/core/admin traffic
   - No API abstraction layer — services directly inject `HttpClient` and construct URLs
   - Each service manually attaches auth headers
   - **For Supabase:** Would need to either:
     - Create a `SupabaseClientService` wrapper and wire it into each feature service, OR
     - Replace HTTP calls with Supabase client library calls (e.g., `this.supabase.from('rfq_items').select(...)`)

3. **Endpoint Shape Expectations**
   - RFQ calculations depend on specific backend JSON shapes:
     - `duty_rate` may arrive as `{ title: number }` or raw number — code defensively checks both
     - Vendor quote arrays use capital `Vendors` (not `vendors`)
     - `req_status` integer values (`null`, `1`, `2`) encode quote state machine
     - Presigned URL flow expects endpoint `/getPreSignedUrl` + `/setObjectPublic`
   - **For Supabase:** Direct REST API calls would match Supabase's table schema exactly; no transformation layer, so schema changes break the client

4. **Master-Detail Grid Coupling**
   - RFQ detail grid columns are fetched per expansion: `POST /getRFQViewlayout { grid_id: 'rfq_detail' }`
   - If Supabase realtime subscriptions are used, column metadata would need its own Supabase table + subscription lifecycle
   - Current caching with `shareReplay()` assumes bounded, infrequent fetches; realtime could create unexpected socket overhead

5. **Reference Data Cache Strategy**
   - `refCache` in `main.service.ts` assumes:
     - Lists are static or rarely change during a session
     - Mutations call `clearRefCache(key)` to invalidate
     - Error failures evict the entry (retry on next read)
   - **For Supabase realtime:** Would need to subscribe to reference tables + keep cache hot, replacing the per-request memoization model

6. **No Direct Supabase Dependency**
   - `package.json` has no `@supabase/supabase-js`
   - All HTTP calls use Angular `HttpClient`
   - Switching to Supabase would require:
     - Adding `@supabase/supabase-js` to dependencies
     - Rewriting service methods from `this.http.get/post/patch` to `this.supabase.from().select()`
     - Handling Supabase RLS (row-level security) policies — JWT must contain correct claims
     - Testing realtime subscriptions + unsubscribe cleanup in component `ngOnDestroy`

7. **HTTP Interceptor Stack**
   - `UserAuthInterceptor` (first) — adds `Authorization` header
   - `LoaderInterceptor` (second) — respects `X-Enable-Loader` header
   - `HttpErrorInterceptor` (third) — catches errors, shows snackbar
   - **For Supabase:** Realtime subscriptions do not flow through HTTP interceptors; auth must be attached to the client instance instead

8. **Large RFQ Component Complexity** (4518 lines)
   - Master grid initialization: 
     - Fetch column headers (`getGridHeader()`)
     - Fetch row data (`getAllItem()`)
     - Restore saved view layout (`getCustomGridLayout()`)
     - Three operations are parallelized via `forkJoin` (added 2026-06-22)
   - **For Supabase:** Would need to fetch from Supabase tables + subscriptions (columns table, items table, layout table) — potentially three separate `supabase.from()` calls

9. **Deferred Performance Work** (HANDOFF.md §A)
   - RFQ grid's backend `getAllItem` returns unbounded rows (~3000+)
   - **Breaking change risk:** If migrating to Supabase, must pair with SSRM (server-side row model) migration
   - Current client-side row model loses filtering/sorting across full dataset if pagination is added without SSRM

10. **Shared DB / Supabase Migration Context**
    - This repo includes `shared-db/` (synced from `u2giants/shared-db`)
    - Shared DB documents unified schema for DAM, CRM, PM, and PLM on Supabase
    - Designflow PLM tables are documented in `shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md`
    - Migrations are staged in `shared-db/supabase/migrations/` (rehearsal-only; not yet applied to live Supabase)
    - **Status:** Schema design is **underway**, but Designflow frontend has NOT yet been updated to use Supabase

---

## Summary

The Designflow frontend is a tightly-coupled, calculation-heavy Angular SPA that orchestrates RFQ pricing, inventory, and tracking workflows via a multi-service REST API. **It has zero direct Supabase dependency today.** A GCP Cloud SQL → Supabase migration would require:

1. **Schema alignment:** Ensure Supabase tables match the JSON shapes the frontend expects (especially RFQ, vendor quote, duty-rate structures)
2. **Service rewrites:** Either wrap Supabase client into each existing service, or rewrite ~120 methods from `HttpClient` to `@supabase/supabase-js`
3. **Auth attachment:** Supabase client needs the same JWT; ensure RLS policies trust the JWT claims (`user_id`, `role`)
4. **Reference data caching:** Replicate `refCache` logic or use Supabase realtime subscriptions (with careful unsubscribe cleanup)
5. **RFQ grid pagination:** Pair any Supabase backend load-balancing with SSRM migration (not implemented yet)
6. **File storage:** Retarget presigned-URL flow to Supabase Storage (if using that) or keep DigitalOcean Spaces
7. **HTTP interceptor assumptions:** Realtime subscriptions bypass interceptors; auth must be baked into Supabase client config

The frontend itself is **low-risk for the migration** (it's just a consumer of APIs); the backend contract changes and schema shape dependencies are the critical unknowns.

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have all the information I need. Let me create the final comprehensive report.

---

# PLM Tracking Service: GCP Cloud SQL → Supabase Migration Analysis

## 1. Purpose & responsibility

**designflow-tracking** is a Node/Express + Sequelize backend service in the Designflow PLM platform, serving the **tracking domain** through the BFF at `/api/tracking/*`. It owns four distinct areas:

- **Production tracking** — production order visibility and date updates; vendor/factory-facing order date entry
- **Licensing tracking** — design licensing rows, status workflow, milestone images, and grouping
- **Lead-time calculation** — design team, licensing, and factory days templates with calculated needed-date backwards logic
- **Sample tracking** (primary focus) — physical sample item tracking: factory-to-Ningbo inbound, NYC-to-factory outbound, and Ningbo-to-customer direct shipments; includes events, photos, grouping, box/shipment management, and state machine

Sample tracking is new on `sandbox-albert` and represents the expansion of tracking beyond production/licensing into physical goods movement.

**File:** `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` (§1)

---

## 2. Tech stack

| Component | Details |
|---|---|
| **Runtime** | Node.js (CommonJS, not ES modules) |
| **Framework** | Express 4.22.1 |
| **ORM** | Sequelize 6.37.3 |
| **Database driver** | pg (node-postgres) 8.11.5 |
| **Database target** | PostgreSQL via `DB_HOST`/`DB_PORT` (currently GCP Cloud SQL, target is Supabase) |
| **Auth** | JWT RS256 via `helpers/auth.js`; BFF passes user JWT as `X-User-Authorization` header, remapped to `Authorization` in `index.js` |
| **File storage** | DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible) via `@aws-sdk/client-s3` for sample photo uploads |
| **Testing** | Jest 29.7.0 (unit tests); Playwright 1.59.1 (e2e configured, not active) |
| **CI/CD** | Cloud Build (GCP) → Cloud Run (private, `--no-allow-unauthenticated`) |

**Files:** `package.json`, `index.js`, `config/`, `Dockerfile`, `cloudbuild.yaml`

---

## 3. Database access

### Connection & Pooling

The service connects to a **pooled/managed PostgreSQL** (currently GCP Cloud SQL, migrating to Supabase) over TCP (no Unix socket). Connection pool settings are **critical** because Supabase's pooler silently closes idle client connections; stale half-open sockets cause 10–26s TCP retransmission stalls and proxy timeouts.

**Current Sequelize pool config** (`models/db.js` lines 24–38):

```javascript
pool: {
    max: 22,
    min: 0,
    acquire: 20000,
    idle: 10000,
    evict: 5000,
},
dialectOptions: {
    connectTimeout: 15000,
    keepAlive: true,
    keepAliveInitialDelayMillis: 10000,
}
```

| Setting | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `pool.min` | `0` | Never hold idle connections (they go stale behind Supabase pooler) |
| `pool.idle` | `10000` (10s) | Evict connections before pooler drops them (typically 30s) |
| `pool.evict` | `5000` (5s) | Run idle-eviction sweep frequently |
| `pool.acquire` | `20000` (20s) | Fail fast; stays **below BFF 30s proxy timeout** |
| `pool.max` | `22` | Per-instance connection ceiling |
| `connectTimeout` | `15000` (15s) | TCP connect cap under BFF timeout |
| `keepAlive` | `true` | OS TCP keep-alive detects dead sockets immediately |
| `keepAliveInitialDelayMillis` | `10000` | Delay before first keep-alive probe |

**Known incident:** SAMPLE-LIST-001 — intermittent `/sample/list` 502 errors when idle intervals exceeded pooler drop time; fix verified in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

**Connection requirements:**
- `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME` (validated by `config/db.config.js`)
- `SCHEMA` env var (e.g. `designflow`) — used in all queries

### Schema & Models

**No separate migration tool.** Sequelize models are the **schema source of truth**:
- `sequelize.sync()` creates new tables from model definitions
- Additive column changes go in idempotent `applySampleMigrations()` block (`ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`, snake_case only)

**35 Sequelize models** in `models/db/*.js`:

**Sample tracking** (7 tables, new on this branch):
- `sample` — primary sample record; links to `itemHeader` (item_id_fk) and `ProdOrderHeader` (prod_order_no_fk); customer shipments via `customer_id_fk` (existing core `customers` table)
- `sample_event` — append-only check-in/out/ship/receive/status_change events
- `sample_comments` — TEXT comments (threaded via `parent_id`); separate from shared `comments` table
- `sample_attachment` — photo metadata; `attachment_link` = DigitalOcean Spaces public URL
- `sample_factory_group` — grouping for factory shipments
- `sample_box` — Ningbo↔NYC shipment container; status independent of member samples
- `sample_shipment_item` — sample↔box membership per leg (factory_to_ningbo, ningbo_to_nyc, nyc_to_ningbo, ningbo_to_factory)

**Shared PLM core** (28 tables, owned by external services or synced):
- `itemHeader`, `itemDetail`, `itemAttachment`, `itemLicenseImage` — item master (synced by `designflow-data-syncing`)
- `ProdOrderHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` — production orders (synced)
- `merchGroup`, `groups`, `users` — cross-domain references
- `LicenseFeedBacks`, `LicensingTime`, `DesignTeamTime`, `FactoryTime` — lead-time templates
- `licensingStatus`, `licensingMilestone` — licensing tracking
- `GridAccessLevel`, `GridLayout`, `GridCellNote` — grid metadata
- `comments`, `itemAttachment`, `deliveryLocation`, `externalCustomer`, `externalVendor`, `UDFTable`, `ProdPaymentTerms`, `ProdShipmentTransitTime`, `ShippingPort` — shared

**No explicit FK constraints.** Foreign keys are application-enforced; the model layer (`Sample.remove`, etc.) owns cascade/integrity.

**Transactions:** No explicit Sequelize transaction usage detected in models; operations are single-statement or rely on DB atomicity.

**Models population:** `models/db.js` populates the export **synchronously** before any `await` (critical quirk: `index.js` calls `db.LicenseFeedBacks.findAll()` synchronously right after `require('./models/db')`).

**Files:** `models/db.js` (init), `models/db/*.js` (35 files), `AGENTS.md` (§3–6), `docs/configuration.md`

---

## 4. Auth

**Mechanism:** JWT RS256 via public key in `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` env var.

**Request flow:**
1. Browser → BFF with user JWT in `Authorization` header
2. BFF replaces `Authorization` with Cloud Run OIDC token, forwards user JWT as `X-User-Authorization` header
3. `index.js` middleware (lines 8–14) remaps `X-User-Authorization` back to `Authorization`
4. Route guards call `authRole(roles)` from `helpers/auth.js`

**Auth implementation** (`helpers/auth.js`):
- `authToken(req, res, next)` — verify JWT, decode into `req.user`
- `authRole(roles)` — gate route; check `decoded.role` against allowed roles (admin bypasses all checks)
- `getUserInfo(token)` — extract user from JWT

**Role inventory:**
- Core roles: `admin`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `designer`, `sales`, `vendor`
- Sample routes define access as: Read (admin, production, sourcing_manager, designer, sales, vendor), Create (admin, production, sourcing_manager, vendor), Write (admin, production, sourcing_manager)

**Files:** `helpers/auth.js`, `index.js` (lines 8–14), `routes/sample.router.js`

---

## 5. External integrations

### DigitalOcean Spaces (Sample Photos)

**S3-compatible object storage** for sample photos. Flow:
1. `POST /sample/getPreSignedUrl` → `helpers/digitalocean.js` generates 15-minute signed PUT URL
2. Browser uploads directly to Spaces
3. `POST /sample/setObjectPublic` → flips ACL to `public-read`, returns CDN URL
4. `POST /sample/photos` → stores URL in `sample_attachment.attachment_link`
5. `DELETE /sample/photos/:attId` → deletes from bucket and DB

**Config** (`config/digitalocean.config.js`):
- `DO_ACCESS_KEY`, `DO_SECRET_KEY` (secrets)
- `DO_ENDPOINT_URL` (default: `https://sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`)
- `DO_BUCKET_NAME` (default: `dflowbucket`)
- `DO_PUBLIC_URL` (default: `https://dflowbucket.sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`)

**Implementation** (`helpers/digitalocean.js`):
- AWS SDK v3 S3 client
- Methods: `getPreSignedUrl()`, `setObjectPublic()`, `deleteObjectsFromBucket()`
- Mirrors `designflow-backend/helpers/digitalocean.js`

### Lead-Time Calculation (Startup Caches)

`index.js` startup (lines 44–94) pre-builds caches for lead-time calculation:
- `LicenseFeedBacksCache` — all feedback records (startup log)
- `LicensingTimeCache` — licensing time templates for fuzzy-matching against mg_desc
- `MgLicTimesMap` — mg_code → LicensingTime mapping (fuzzy-matched at startup)
- `MgFactoryTimesMap` — mg_code → FactoryTime mapping (fuzzy-matched at startup)

Used by `/lead-time/calculate` endpoint to derive needed dates backwards from delivery dates.

**Files:** `index.js` (lines 44–94), `helpers/leadTimeUtils.js`

### No Email, AI, Webhooks, or Cron

Searches for `email`, `smtp`, `webhook`, `cron`, `queue`, `stripe` found no implementation. Sample tracking is **synchronous request/response only**.

---

## 6. API surface

All routes sit behind JWT auth (except health). Called via BFF `/api/tracking/*` prefix.

### Health

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/api/health` | none | Service health |

### Production Tracking (root `/`)

`routes/lib.router.js`, `controllers/main.controller.js`, `models/lib.model.js`

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/getProdOrderHeader` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Grid rows |
| GET | `/getViewlayout/:grid_id` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Layout |
| POST | `/updateProdOrderHeader` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Update row |
| GET | `/factoryProdOrders` | vendor | Factory-visible orders |
| POST | `/factoryProdOrderDates` | vendor | Update factory dates |
| POST | `/syncPrepackAssociations` | production | Sync prepack |

### Licensing Tracking (`/lic`)

`routes/lic.router.js`, `controllers/lic.controller.js`, `models/lic.model.js`

18 routes covering grid rows, statuses, images, and group management.

### Lead-Time (`/lead-time`)

`routes/lead-time.router.js`, `controllers/lead-time.controller.js`, `models/leadTimeUtils.js`

6 routes: read templates, calculate, update templates (internal roles only).

### Sample Tracking (`/sample`)

`routes/sample.router.js`, `controllers/sample*.controller.js`, `models/sample*.model.js`

38 routes total across 4 controllers:
- **sample.controller.js** — CRUD, events, list (grid-based pagination)
- **sampleComment.controller.js** — create, list, update, delete comments
- **sampleGroup.controller.js** — factory group and box CRUD
- **sampleMedia.controller.js** — photo presigned URL, public ACL, metadata, delete

**Key sample routes:**
| Method | Path | Auth group | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/sample/list` | Read | Grid pagination (AG Grid compatible) |
| POST | `/sample` | Create | Create sample |
| POST | `/sample/customer` | Write | Create Ningbo-to-customer sample |
| PUT | `/sample/:id` | Write | Update sample |
| DELETE | `/sample/:id` | Write | Delete sample |
| POST | `/sample/events` | Write | Record check-in/out/ship/status-change |
| GET | `/sample/:id/events` | Read | List events (append-only) |
| POST/GET/PUT/DELETE | `/sample/comments*` | Read/Read/Read/Read | Comment threads |
| POST | `/sample/getPreSignedUrl` | Write | 15-min upload URL |
| POST | `/sample/setObjectPublic` | Write | Make photo public, return CDN URL |
| GET | `/sample/:id/photos` | Read | List photos |
| POST/DELETE | `/sample/photos*` | Write | Photo metadata + delete |
| GET/POST/PUT/DELETE | `/sample/groups*` | Read/Write | Factory groups |
| GET/POST/PUT/DELETE | `/sample/boxes*` | Read/Write | Box CRUD |
| POST/DELETE | `/sample/boxes/:id/items` | Write | Add/remove samples to/from box |

**Grid contract:** `?startRow`, `?endRow`, `?filterModel` (JSON) → `{ rows, totalCount }` (AG Grid compatible).

**Files:** `routes/index.js`, `routes/sample.router.js`, `controllers/sample*.js`, `docs/api-reference.md`

---

## 7. Sample tracking — presence & confirmation

**All 7 sample* tables CONFIRMED PRESENT on sandbox-albert branch:**

1. **sample** — 107 lines; primary sample record with origin, direction, status, office_location, qty, pricing, courier, factory/item/order links
2. **sample_event** — 71 lines; append-only events (check_in, check_out, ship, receive, status_change); supports sample/box/factory_group targets
3. **sample_comments** — 46 lines; threaded TEXT comments (separate from shared `comments` table; allows longer text)
4. **sample_attachment** — 70 lines; photo metadata; `attachment_link` = public Spaces URL
5. **sample_factory_group** — 55 lines; grouping for factory shipments
6. **sample_box** — 63 lines; Ningbo↔NYC shipment container; independent status
7. **sample_shipment_item** — 47 lines; sample↔box membership per leg (factory_to_ningbo, ningbo_to_nyc, etc.)

**Associated models:**
- `models/sample.model.js` (151 lines) — list (grid pagination), getById, create, update, remove, recordEvent, listEvents
- `models/sampleComment.model.js` — comment CRUD
- `models/sampleGroup.model.js` — Group (factory_group) and Box CRUD
- `models/sampleMedia.model.js` — photo workflow (presigned URL, public ACL, metadata, delete)

**Supporting:**
- `helpers/sampleStatus.js` (140 lines) — state machine: origin → direction → transitions; validates status moves; shared extras (cancelled, on_hold, damaged, approved); terminal extras (cancelled)
- `controllers/sample*.js` (4 files) — thin HTTP handlers

**Files:** `models/db/sample*.js` (7), `models/sample*.model.js` (4), `helpers/sampleStatus.js`, `controllers/sample*.controller.js` (4)

---

## 8. Heavy business logic

### Lead-Time Calculation

**Sequelize startup caches** (`index.js` lines 44–94):
- Loads all `LicenseFeedBacks`, `LicensingTime`, `DesignTeamTime`, `FactoryTime` into global memory
- Pre-builds mg_code → LicensingTime/FactoryTime maps by fuzzy-matching against mg_desc
- Avoids repeated fuzzy matching on every request

**Calculation flow:** `/lead-time/calculate` uses `LicensingTimeCache`, `MgLicTimesMap`, `MgFactoryTimesMap` to derive needed date backwards from delivery date.

**State machine risk:** Fuzzy matching logic in `helpers/leadTimeUtils.js` must be preserved across migration. Custom code, not data.

### Sample Status State Machine

**Core logic** (`helpers/sampleStatus.js`, 140 lines):
- Four origins (`factory_request`, `factory_offer`, `usa_bought`, `customer`) → two directions (`inbound`, `outbound`, `customer`)
- Per-direction ordered backbone statuses (5–7 states each)
- Five event types (`check_in`, `check_out`, `ship`, `receive`, `status_change`) trigger deterministic transitions
- Shared extra states (`cancelled`, `on_hold`, `damaged`, `approved`) reachable any time; `cancelled` is terminal
- Resume-from-hold back onto backbone

**Implementation** (`models/sample.model.js`, `helpers/sampleStatus.js`):
- `recordEvent()` calls `deriveToStatus()` to compute next status from (direction, current, office, event_type)
- Updates sample with optimistic guard (`WHERE status=<from_status>`, 0 rows → 409 conflict)
- Appends `sample_event` row (audit trail)

**Files:** `helpers/sampleStatus.js`, `models/sample.model.js` (recordEvent, ~150 lines)

### Grid Pagination & Filtering

AG Grid-compatible filter model on `/sample/list`:
- Server-side pagination: `startRow`, `endRow` → LIMIT/OFFSET
- Filter model: JSON; supports date, number, text filters (iLike case-insensitive, contains, equals, etc.)
- Whitelist guards (`FILTERABLE` set) prevent SQL injection via colId

Mirrors production/licensing grid filtering in `lib.model.js` and `lic.model.js`.

**Files:** `models/sample.model.js` (buildCondition, list, ~50 lines)

### Derived Fields

`GET /sample/list` returns two computed (non-stored) fields per row:
- `primary_photo` — the sample's primary `sample_attachment.attachment_link` (if exists)
- `box_label` — the `sample_box.box_label` for the sample's current `box_id_fk` (if exists)

Computed on read, not stored.

**Files:** `models/sample.model.js` (list method)

---

## 9. Migration-relevant observations

### GCP Cloud SQL Coupling

**Current setup:**
- `cloudbuild.yaml`: GCP Cloud Build (steps 1–3) → Artifact Registry → Cloud Run
- Secrets from GCP Secret Manager (`projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/...`)
- Env vars via Cloud Build substitutions (`_REGION`, `_SERVICE_NAME`, etc.)
- No Cloud SQL unix socket; TCP connection over VPC connector

**Migration impact:**
- DB credentials (HOST, PORT, USER, PASSWORD, NAME) move from GCP Secret Manager to Supabase project
- Connection pool settings already tuned for **pooled/managed Postgres** (current config works for Supabase without change)
- VPC connector: Supabase has its own IP; may require firewall/network review
- **No GCP SDK calls in code** — no hard dependencies beyond Cloud Build deployment

**Files:** `cloudbuild.yaml`, `config/db.config.js`, `models/db.js`

### Environment Variables

**Database (required for boot):**
- `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`
- `SCHEMA` (e.g. `designflow`)

**Auth:**
- `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` (required)
- `JWT_PRIVATE_KEY` (not used; present for completeness)

**DigitalOcean Spaces (photo uploads):**
- `DO_ACCESS_KEY`, `DO_SECRET_KEY` (secrets; present on Cloud Run)
- `DO_ENDPOINT_URL` (default: `https://sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`)
- `DO_BUCKET_NAME` (default: `dflowbucket`)
- `DO_PUBLIC_URL` (default: `https://dflowbucket.sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`)

**Context/logging:**
- `WEB_URL`, `API_ADDRESS`, `SCHEMA`, `PORT` (optional; for startup logs)

**Mapping:** `config/db.config.js` (DB vars), `config/digitalocean.config.js` (DO vars), `config/env.js` (.env.${NODE_ENV}).

**Files:** `config/`, `docs/configuration.md`

### Model Duplication & Shared Ownership

**Shared tables across services:**
- `itemHeader`, `itemDetail`, `itemAttachment`, `itemLicenseImage` — owned by `designflow-data-syncing` (Coldlion sync); read by tracking
- `ProdOrderHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` — synced; both tracking and other PLM services read
- `merchGroup`, `groups`, `users` — read-only references for tracking
- `comments` (shared) vs `sample_comments` (tracking-local) — intentional split (shared table caps at 50 chars; sample comments use TEXT)
- `externalCustomer`, `externalVendor` — read-only references

**Intentional isolation:**
- Sample tracking uses dedicated `sample*` tables, NOT `itemHeader` rows
- Reason: `itemHeader` is externally synced; injecting samples would pollute the item master and risk samples appearing as real items
- Links via `sample.item_id_fk`, `sample.prod_order_no_fk` instead (application-enforced)

**Risk:** Shared tables are synced by `designflow-data-syncing` and read by multiple services. Supabase migration must coordinate schema changes across all consumers.

**Files:** `AGENTS.md` (§9), `docs/architecture.md` (§8–9)

### Pool Settings & Supabase-Specific Tuning

**Critical:** Supabase pgBouncer pooler silently closes idle connections; stale half-open sockets cause intermittent 502 errors.

**Current settings already handle this:**
```javascript
pool: { min: 0, idle: 10000, evict: 5000, acquire: 20000, max: 22 }
dialectOptions: { connectTimeout: 15000, keepAlive: true, keepAliveInitialDelayMillis: 10000 }
```

**Do not loosen:**
- Keep `min: 0` (don't hold idle connections)
- Keep `idle: 10000` (evict before pooler drops them at ~30s)
- Keep `acquire: 20000` (fail fast, stays below BFF 30s timeout)
- Keep `keepAlive: true` (detect dead sockets fast)

**Known incident:** SAMPLE-LIST-001 — intermittent timeout on `/sample/list` when idle exceeded pooler drop time. Guarded by `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

**Files:** `models/db.js` (lines 24–38), `docs/configuration.md` (§9), `AGENTS.md` (§9)

### Sequelize & Migration Approach

**Schema source of truth:** Sequelize models, not separate migrations.

- `sequelize.sync()` creates new tables from `models/db/*.js` definitions
- Additive columns: idempotent `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` in `applySampleMigrations()` function
- Snake_case only in DDL (unquoted identifiers fold to lowercase in Postgres)

**Supabase consideration:**
- Run `sync()` on first boot to create tables (safe; only creates if missing)
- Supabase migrations ledger is separate; don't commit to it unless coordinating with other apps
- `shared-db/` folder contains shared schema definitions for DAM, CRM, PM, and PLM

**Files:** `models/db.js`, `shared-db/README.md`, `shared-db/docs/`

### Shared Database Context

This repo includes a copy of `shared-db/` (auto-synced from `u2giants/shared-db`). The shared database unifies DAM, CRM, PM, and PLM on Supabase:

- **Canonical source:** `u2giants/shared-db` (not edited here)
- **Migration package:** `shared-db/supabase/migrations/` (foundation, core, domain_tables, RLS/realtime)
- **Preview branch:** `shared-db-schema-rehearsal` (persistent Supabase preview for testing)
- **Coordination:** All four apps (CRM, PM, DAM, PLM/tracking) share the same Supabase project

**Implication:** Tracking's schema changes must be coordinated through `shared-db` AGENTS.md contract. There are strict rules to prevent services from breaking each other.

**Files:** `shared-db/`, `shared-db/README.md`, `shared-db/AGENTS.md`

---

## 10. Testing & Quality Gates

- **Unit tests** — Jest; guard DB migrations, auth, status state machine, grid filtering
- **E2E tests** — Playwright configured but not the active testing path
- **Pre-deploy gate** — `npm test` runs before app startup in all `start:*` scripts; failing tests block deployment
- **Test coverage:** Auth, status state machine, grid layouts, sample model functions verified

**Files:** `tests/unit/`, `jest.config.js`, `tests/e2e/`

---

## Summary: Key Risks & Decisions

| Area | Current | Migration notes |
|---|---|---|
| **DB connection** | GCP Cloud SQL over TCP + VPC connector | Supabase Postgres + pooler; pool config already tuned; no changes needed |
| **Secrets** | GCP Secret Manager | Move to Supabase project secrets; `cloudbuild.yaml` substitutions stay the same |
| **Pool settings** | min=0, idle=10s, evict=5s, acquire=20s, keepAlive=true | **DO NOT LOOSEN**; already Supabase-optimized |
| **Model ownership** | sample* (tracking), shared item/order/user/merch/grid (synced/referenced) | Coordinate schema changes through `shared-db` AGENTS.md |
| **Lead-time caches** | Startup pre-builds mg_code maps from LicensingTime/FactoryTime | Stateless; no migration risk; caches refresh on boot |
| **Sample status SM** | Deterministic (direction, current status, office, event_type) → next status | Custom code; mirror in frontend `pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status.ts`; must stay in sync |
| **DigitalOcean Spaces** | S3-compatible presigned URL + public ACL | No changes needed; same bucket in sandbox + prod (`dflowbucket`) |
| **Shared database** | Consolidating DAM, CRM, PM, PLM into one Supabase project | Track `shared-db/AGENTS.md` for cross-service coordination; four-app merge protocol |

---

**Files examined:** 60+ source files; key: `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `index.js`, `models/db.js`, `models/db/*.js` (35), `models/sample*.model.js` (4), `helpers/sampleStatus.js`, `config/`, `routes/`, `controllers/`, `docs/`, `cloudbuild.yaml`, `package.json`

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have all the information I need. Let me create the final comprehensive report.

---

# PLM Tracking Service: GCP Cloud SQL → Supabase Migration Analysis

## 1. Purpose & responsibility

**designflow-tracking** is a Node/Express + Sequelize backend service in the Designflow PLM platform, serving the **tracking domain** through the BFF at `/api/tracking/*`. It owns four distinct areas:

- **Production tracking** — production order visibility and date updates; vendor/factory-facing order date entry
- **Licensing tracking** — design licensing rows, status workflow, milestone images, and grouping
- **Lead-time calculation** — design team, licensing, and factory days templates with calculated needed-date backwards logic
- **Sample tracking** (primary focus) — physical sample item tracking: factory-to-Ningbo inbound, NYC-to-factory outbound, and Ningbo-to-customer direct shipments; includes events, photos, grouping, box/shipment management, and state machine

Sample tracking is new on `sandbox-albert` and represents the expansion of tracking beyond production/licensing into physical goods movement.

**File:** `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` (§1)

---

## 2. Tech stack

| Component | Details |
|---|---|
| **Runtime** | Node.js (CommonJS, not ES modules) |
| **Framework** | Express 4.22.1 |
| **ORM** | Sequelize 6.37.3 |
| **Database driver** | pg (node-postgres) 8.11.5 |
| **Database target** | PostgreSQL via `DB_HOST`/`DB_PORT` (currently GCP Cloud SQL, target is Supabase) |
| **Auth** | JWT RS256 via `helpers/auth.js`; BFF passes user JWT as `X-User-Authorization` header, remapped to `Authorization` in `index.js` |
| **File storage** | DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible) via `@aws-sdk/client-s3` for sample photo uploads |
| **Testing** | Jest 29.7.0 (unit tests); Playwright 1.59.1 (e2e configured, not active) |
| **CI/CD** | Cloud Build (GCP) → Cloud Run (private, `--no-allow-unauthenticated`) |

**Files:** `package.json`, `index.js`, `config/`, `Dockerfile`, `cloudbuild.yaml`

---

## 3. Database access

### Connection & Pooling

The service connects to a **pooled/managed PostgreSQL** (currently GCP Cloud SQL, migrating to Supabase) over TCP (no Unix socket). Connection pool settings are **critical** because Supabase's pooler silently closes idle client connections; stale half-open sockets cause 10–26s TCP retransmission stalls and proxy timeouts.

**Current Sequelize pool config** (`models/db.js` lines 24–38):

```javascript
pool: {
    max: 22,
    min: 0,
    acquire: 20000,
    idle: 10000,
    evict: 5000,
},
dialectOptions: {
    connectTimeout: 15000,
    keepAlive: true,
    keepAliveInitialDelayMillis: 10000,
}
```

| Setting | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `pool.min` | `0` | Never hold idle connections (they go stale behind Supabase pooler) |
| `pool.idle` | `10000` (10s) | Evict connections before pooler drops them (typically 30s) |
| `pool.evict` | `5000` (5s) | Run idle-eviction sweep frequently |
| `pool.acquire` | `20000` (20s) | Fail fast; stays **below BFF 30s proxy timeout** |
| `pool.max` | `22` | Per-instance connection ceiling |
| `connectTimeout` | `15000` (15s) | TCP connect cap under BFF timeout |
| `keepAlive` | `true` | OS TCP keep-alive detects dead sockets immediately |
| `keepAliveInitialDelayMillis` | `10000` | Delay before first keep-alive probe |

**Known incident:** SAMPLE-LIST-001 — intermittent `/sample/list` 502 errors when idle intervals exceeded pooler drop time; fix verified in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

**Connection requirements:**
- `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME` (validated by `config/db.config.js`)
- `SCHEMA` env var (e.g. `designflow`) — used in all queries

### Schema & Models

**No separate migration tool.** Sequelize models are the **schema source of truth**:
- `sequelize.sync()` creates new tables from model definitions
- Additive column changes go in idempotent `applySampleMigrations()` block (`ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`, snake_case only)

**35 Sequelize models** in `models/db/*.js`:

**Sample tracking** (7 tables, new on this branch):
- `sample` — primary sample record; links to `itemHeader` (item_id_fk) and `ProdOrderHeader` (prod_order_no_fk); customer shipments via `customer_id_fk` (existing core `customers` table)
- `sample_event` — append-only check-in/out/ship/receive/status_change events
- `sample_comments` — TEXT comments (threaded via `parent_id`); separate from shared `comments` table
- `sample_attachment` — photo metadata; `attachment_link` = DigitalOcean Spaces public URL
- `sample_factory_group` — grouping for factory shipments
- `sample_box` — Ningbo↔NYC shipment container; status independent of member samples
- `sample_shipment_item` — sample↔box membership per leg (factory_to_ningbo, ningbo_to_nyc, nyc_to_ningbo, ningbo_to_factory)

**Shared PLM core** (28 tables, owned by external services or synced):
- `itemHeader`, `itemDetail`, `itemAttachment`, `itemLicenseImage` — item master (synced by `designflow-data-syncing`)
- `ProdOrderHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` — production orders (synced)
- `merchGroup`, `groups`, `users` — cross-domain references
- `LicenseFeedBacks`, `LicensingTime`, `DesignTeamTime`, `FactoryTime` — lead-time templates
- `licensingStatus`, `licensingMilestone` — licensing tracking
- `GridAccessLevel`, `GridLayout`, `GridCellNote` — grid metadata
- `comments`, `itemAttachment`, `deliveryLocation`, `externalCustomer`, `externalVendor`, `UDFTable`, `ProdPaymentTerms`, `ProdShipmentTransitTime`, `ShippingPort` — shared

**No explicit FK constraints.** Foreign keys are application-enforced; the model layer (`Sample.remove`, etc.) owns cascade/integrity.

**Transactions:** No explicit Sequelize transaction usage detected in models; operations are single-statement or rely on DB atomicity.

**Models population:** `models/db.js` populates the export **synchronously** before any `await` (critical quirk: `index.js` calls `db.LicenseFeedBacks.findAll()` synchronously right after `require('./models/db')`).

**Files:** `models/db.js` (init), `models/db/*.js` (35 files), `AGENTS.md` (§3–6), `docs/configuration.md`

---

## 4. Auth

**Mechanism:** JWT RS256 via public key in `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` env var.

**Request flow:**
1. Browser → BFF with user JWT in `Authorization` header
2. BFF replaces `Authorization` with Cloud Run OIDC token, forwards user JWT as `X-User-Authorization` header
3. `index.js` middleware (lines 8–14) remaps `X-User-Authorization` back to `Authorization`
4. Route guards call `authRole(roles)` from `helpers/auth.js`

**Auth implementation** (`helpers/auth.js`):
- `authToken(req, res, next)` — verify JWT, decode into `req.user`
- `authRole(roles)` — gate route; check `decoded.role` against allowed roles (admin bypasses all checks)
- `getUserInfo(token)` — extract user from JWT

**Role inventory:**
- Core roles: `admin`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `designer`, `sales`, `vendor`
- Sample routes define access as: Read (admin, production, sourcing_manager, designer, sales, vendor), Create (admin, production, sourcing_manager, vendor), Write (admin, production, sourcing_manager)

**Files:** `helpers/auth.js`, `index.js` (lines 8–14), `routes/sample.router.js`

---

## 5. External integrations

### DigitalOcean Spaces (Sample Photos)

**S3-compatible object storage** for sample photos. Flow:
1. `POST /sample/getPreSignedUrl` → `helpers/digitalocean.js` generates 15-minute signed PUT URL
2. Browser uploads directly to Spaces
3. `POST /sample/setObjectPublic` → flips ACL to `public-read`, returns CDN URL
4. `POST /sample/photos` → stores URL in `sample_attachment.attachment_link`
5. `DELETE /sample/photos/:attId` → deletes from bucket and DB

**Config** (`config/digitalocean.config.js`):
- `DO_ACCESS_KEY`, `DO_SECRET_KEY` (secrets)
- `DO_ENDPOINT_URL` (default: `https://sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`)
- `DO_BUCKET_NAME` (default: `dflowbucket`)
- `DO_PUBLIC_URL` (default: `https://dflowbucket.sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`)

**Implementation** (`helpers/digitalocean.js`):
- AWS SDK v3 S3 client
- Methods: `getPreSignedUrl()`, `setObjectPublic()`, `deleteObjectsFromBucket()`
- Mirrors `designflow-backend/helpers/digitalocean.js`

### Lead-Time Calculation (Startup Caches)

`index.js` startup (lines 44–94) pre-builds caches for lead-time calculation:
- `LicenseFeedBacksCache` — all feedback records (startup log)
- `LicensingTimeCache` — licensing time templates for fuzzy-matching against mg_desc
- `MgLicTimesMap` — mg_code → LicensingTime mapping (fuzzy-matched at startup)
- `MgFactoryTimesMap` — mg_code → FactoryTime mapping (fuzzy-matched at startup)

Used by `/lead-time/calculate` endpoint to derive needed dates backwards from delivery dates.

**Files:** `index.js` (lines 44–94), `helpers/leadTimeUtils.js`

### No Email, AI, Webhooks, or Cron

Searches for `email`, `smtp`, `webhook`, `cron`, `queue`, `stripe` found no implementation. Sample tracking is **synchronous request/response only**.

---

## 6. API surface

All routes sit behind JWT auth (except health). Called via BFF `/api/tracking/*` prefix.

### Health

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/api/health` | none | Service health |

### Production Tracking (root `/`)

`routes/lib.router.js`, `controllers/main.controller.js`, `models/lib.model.js`

| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/getProdOrderHeader` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Grid rows |
| GET | `/getViewlayout/:grid_id` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Layout |
| POST | `/updateProdOrderHeader` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Update row |
| GET | `/factoryProdOrders` | vendor | Factory-visible orders |
| POST | `/factoryProdOrderDates` | vendor | Update factory dates |
| POST | `/syncPrepackAssociations` | production | Sync prepack |

### Licensing Tracking (`/lic`)

`routes/lic.router.js`, `controllers/lic.controller.js`, `models/lic.model.js`

18 routes covering grid rows, statuses, images, and group management.

### Lead-Time (`/lead-time`)

`routes/lead-time.router.js`, `controllers/lead-time.controller.js`, `models/leadTimeUtils.js`

6 routes: read templates, calculate, update templates (internal roles only).

### Sample Tracking (`/sample`)

`routes/sample.router.js`, `controllers/sample*.controller.js`, `models/sample*.model.js`

38 routes total across 4 controllers:
- **sample.controller.js** — CRUD, events, list (grid-based pagination)
- **sampleComment.controller.js** — create, list, update, delete comments
- **sampleGroup.controller.js** — factory group and box CRUD
- **sampleMedia.controller.js** — photo presigned URL, public ACL, metadata, delete

**Key sample routes:**
| Method | Path | Auth group | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/sample/list` | Read | Grid pagination (AG Grid compatible) |
| POST | `/sample` | Create | Create sample |
| POST | `/sample/customer` | Write | Create Ningbo-to-customer sample |
| PUT | `/sample/:id` | Write | Update sample |
| DELETE | `/sample/:id` | Write | Delete sample |
| POST | `/sample/events` | Write | Record check-in/out/ship/status-change |
| GET | `/sample/:id/events` | Read | List events (append-only) |
| POST/GET/PUT/DELETE | `/sample/comments*` | Read/Read/Read/Read | Comment threads |
| POST | `/sample/getPreSignedUrl` | Write | 15-min upload URL |
| POST | `/sample/setObjectPublic` | Write | Make photo public, return CDN URL |
| GET | `/sample/:id/photos` | Read | List photos |
| POST/DELETE | `/sample/photos*` | Write | Photo metadata + delete |
| GET/POST/PUT/DELETE | `/sample/groups*` | Read/Write | Factory groups |
| GET/POST/PUT/DELETE | `/sample/boxes*` | Read/Write | Box CRUD |
| POST/DELETE | `/sample/boxes/:id/items` | Write | Add/remove samples to/from box |

**Grid contract:** `?startRow`, `?endRow`, `?filterModel` (JSON) → `{ rows, totalCount }` (AG Grid compatible).

**Files:** `routes/index.js`, `routes/sample.router.js`, `controllers/sample*.js`, `docs/api-reference.md`

---

## 7. Sample tracking — presence & confirmation

**All 7 sample* tables CONFIRMED PRESENT on sandbox-albert branch:**

1. **sample** — 107 lines; primary sample record with origin, direction, status, office_location, qty, pricing, courier, factory/item/order links
2. **sample_event** — 71 lines; append-only events (check_in, check_out, ship, receive, status_change); supports sample/box/factory_group targets
3. **sample_comments** — 46 lines; threaded TEXT comments (separate from shared `comments` table; allows longer text)
4. **sample_attachment** — 70 lines; photo metadata; `attachment_link` = public Spaces URL
5. **sample_factory_group** — 55 lines; grouping for factory shipments
6. **sample_box** — 63 lines; Ningbo↔NYC shipment container; independent status
7. **sample_shipment_item** — 47 lines; sample↔box membership per leg (factory_to_ningbo, ningbo_to_nyc, etc.)

**Associated models:**
- `models/sample.model.js` (151 lines) — list (grid pagination), getById, create, update, remove, recordEvent, listEvents
- `models/sampleComment.model.js` — comment CRUD
- `models/sampleGroup.model.js` — Group (factory_group) and Box CRUD
- `models/sampleMedia.model.js` — photo workflow (presigned URL, public ACL, metadata, delete)

**Supporting:**
- `helpers/sampleStatus.js` (140 lines) — state machine: origin → direction → transitions; validates status moves; shared extras (cancelled, on_hold, damaged, approved); terminal extras (cancelled)
- `controllers/sample*.js` (4 files) — thin HTTP handlers

**Files:** `models/db/sample*.js` (7), `models/sample*.model.js` (4), `helpers/sampleStatus.js`, `controllers/sample*.controller.js` (4)

---

## 8. Heavy business logic

### Lead-Time Calculation

**Sequelize startup caches** (`index.js` lines 44–94):
- Loads all `LicenseFeedBacks`, `LicensingTime`, `DesignTeamTime`, `FactoryTime` into global memory
- Pre-builds mg_code → LicensingTime/FactoryTime maps by fuzzy-matching against mg_desc
- Avoids repeated fuzzy matching on every request

**Calculation flow:** `/lead-time/calculate` uses `LicensingTimeCache`, `MgLicTimesMap`, `MgFactoryTimesMap` to derive needed date backwards from delivery date.

**State machine risk:** Fuzzy matching logic in `helpers/leadTimeUtils.js` must be preserved across migration. Custom code, not data.

### Sample Status State Machine

**Core logic** (`helpers/sampleStatus.js`, 140 lines):
- Four origins (`factory_request`, `factory_offer`, `usa_bought`, `customer`) → two directions (`inbound`, `outbound`, `customer`)
- Per-direction ordered backbone statuses (5–7 states each)
- Five event types (`check_in`, `check_out`, `ship`, `receive`, `status_change`) trigger deterministic transitions
- Shared extra states (`cancelled`, `on_hold`, `damaged`, `approved`) reachable any time; `cancelled` is terminal
- Resume-from-hold back onto backbone

**Implementation** (`models/sample.model.js`, `helpers/sampleStatus.js`):
- `recordEvent()` calls `deriveToStatus()` to compute next status from (direction, current, office, event_type)
- Updates sample with optimistic guard (`WHERE status=<from_status>`, 0 rows → 409 conflict)
- Appends `sample_event` row (audit trail)

**Files:** `helpers/sampleStatus.js`, `models/sample.model.js` (recordEvent, ~150 lines)

### Grid Pagination & Filtering

AG Grid-compatible filter model on `/sample/list`:
- Server-side pagination: `startRow`, `endRow` → LIMIT/OFFSET
- Filter model: JSON; supports date, number, text filters (iLike case-insensitive, contains, equals, etc.)
- Whitelist guards (`FILTERABLE` set) prevent SQL injection via colId

Mirrors production/licensing grid filtering in `lib.model.js` and `lic.model.js`.

**Files:** `models/sample.model.js` (buildCondition, list, ~50 lines)

### Derived Fields

`GET /sample/list` returns two computed (non-stored) fields per row:
- `primary_photo` — the sample's primary `sample_attachment.attachment_link` (if exists)
- `box_label` — the `sample_box.box_label` for the sample's current `box_id_fk` (if exists)

Computed on read, not stored.

**Files:** `models/sample.model.js` (list method)

---

## 9. Migration-relevant observations

### GCP Cloud SQL Coupling

**Current setup:**
- `cloudbuild.yaml`: GCP Cloud Build (steps 1–3) → Artifact Registry → Cloud Run
- Secrets from GCP Secret Manager (`projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/...`)
- Env vars via Cloud Build substitutions (`_REGION`, `_SERVICE_NAME`, etc.)
- No Cloud SQL unix socket; TCP connection over VPC connector

**Migration impact:**
- DB credentials (HOST, PORT, USER, PASSWORD, NAME) move from GCP Secret Manager to Supabase project
- Connection pool settings already tuned for **pooled/managed Postgres** (current config works for Supabase without change)
- VPC connector: Supabase has its own IP; may require firewall/network review
- **No GCP SDK calls in code** — no hard dependencies beyond Cloud Build deployment

**Files:** `cloudbuild.yaml`, `config/db.config.js`, `models/db.js`

### Environment Variables

**Database (required for boot):**
- `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`
- `SCHEMA` (e.g. `designflow`)

**Auth:**
- `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` (required)
- `JWT_PRIVATE_KEY` (not used; present for completeness)

**DigitalOcean Spaces (photo uploads):**
- `DO_ACCESS_KEY`, `DO_SECRET_KEY` (secrets; present on Cloud Run)
- `DO_ENDPOINT_URL` (default: `https://sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`)
- `DO_BUCKET_NAME` (default: `dflowbucket`)
- `DO_PUBLIC_URL` (default: `https://dflowbucket.sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`)

**Context/logging:**
- `WEB_URL`, `API_ADDRESS`, `SCHEMA`, `PORT` (optional; for startup logs)

**Mapping:** `config/db.config.js` (DB vars), `config/digitalocean.config.js` (DO vars), `config/env.js` (.env.${NODE_ENV}).

**Files:** `config/`, `docs/configuration.md`

### Model Duplication & Shared Ownership

**Shared tables across services:**
- `itemHeader`, `itemDetail`, `itemAttachment`, `itemLicenseImage` — owned by `designflow-data-syncing` (Coldlion sync); read by tracking
- `ProdOrderHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` — synced; both tracking and other PLM services read
- `merchGroup`, `groups`, `users` — read-only references for tracking
- `comments` (shared) vs `sample_comments` (tracking-local) — intentional split (shared table caps at 50 chars; sample comments use TEXT)
- `externalCustomer`, `externalVendor` — read-only references

**Intentional isolation:**
- Sample tracking uses dedicated `sample*` tables, NOT `itemHeader` rows
- Reason: `itemHeader` is externally synced; injecting samples would pollute the item master and risk samples appearing as real items
- Links via `sample.item_id_fk`, `sample.prod_order_no_fk` instead (application-enforced)

**Risk:** Shared tables are synced by `designflow-data-syncing` and read by multiple services. Supabase migration must coordinate schema changes across all consumers.

**Files:** `AGENTS.md` (§9), `docs/architecture.md` (§8–9)

### Pool Settings & Supabase-Specific Tuning

**Critical:** Supabase pgBouncer pooler silently closes idle connections; stale half-open sockets cause intermittent 502 errors.

**Current settings already handle this:**
```javascript
pool: { min: 0, idle: 10000, evict: 5000, acquire: 20000, max: 22 }
dialectOptions: { connectTimeout: 15000, keepAlive: true, keepAliveInitialDelayMillis: 10000 }
```

**Do not loosen:**
- Keep `min: 0` (don't hold idle connections)
- Keep `idle: 10000` (evict before pooler drops them at ~30s)
- Keep `acquire: 20000` (fail fast, stays below BFF 30s timeout)
- Keep `keepAlive: true` (detect dead sockets fast)

**Known incident:** SAMPLE-LIST-001 — intermittent timeout on `/sample/list` when idle exceeded pooler drop time. Guarded by `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

**Files:** `models/db.js` (lines 24–38), `docs/configuration.md` (§9), `AGENTS.md` (§9)

### Sequelize & Migration Approach

**Schema source of truth:** Sequelize models, not separate migrations.

- `sequelize.sync()` creates new tables from `models/db/*.js` definitions
- Additive columns: idempotent `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` in `applySampleMigrations()` function
- Snake_case only in DDL (unquoted identifiers fold to lowercase in Postgres)

**Supabase consideration:**
- Run `sync()` on first boot to create tables (safe; only creates if missing)
- Supabase migrations ledger is separate; don't commit to it unless coordinating with other apps
- `shared-db/` folder contains shared schema definitions for DAM, CRM, PM, and PLM

**Files:** `models/db.js`, `shared-db/README.md`, `shared-db/docs/`

### Shared Database Context

This repo includes a copy of `shared-db/` (auto-synced from `u2giants/shared-db`). The shared database unifies DAM, CRM, PM, and PLM on Supabase:

- **Canonical source:** `u2giants/shared-db` (not edited here)
- **Migration package:** `shared-db/supabase/migrations/` (foundation, core, domain_tables, RLS/realtime)
- **Preview branch:** `shared-db-schema-rehearsal` (persistent Supabase preview for testing)
- **Coordination:** All four apps (CRM, PM, DAM, PLM/tracking) share the same Supabase project

**Implication:** Tracking's schema changes must be coordinated through `shared-db` AGENTS.md contract. There are strict rules to prevent services from breaking each other.

**Files:** `shared-db/`, `shared-db/README.md`, `shared-db/AGENTS.md`

---

## 10. Testing & Quality Gates

- **Unit tests** — Jest; guard DB migrations, auth, status state machine, grid filtering
- **E2E tests** — Playwright configured but not the active testing path
- **Pre-deploy gate** — `npm test` runs before app startup in all `start:*` scripts; failing tests block deployment
- **Test coverage:** Auth, status state machine, grid layouts, sample model functions verified

**Files:** `tests/unit/`, `jest.config.js`, `tests/e2e/`

---

## Summary: Key Risks & Decisions

| Area | Current | Migration notes |
|---|---|---|
| **DB connection** | GCP Cloud SQL over TCP + VPC connector | Supabase Postgres + pooler; pool config already tuned; no changes needed |
| **Secrets** | GCP Secret Manager | Move to Supabase project secrets; `cloudbuild.yaml` substitutions stay the same |
| **Pool settings** | min=0, idle=10s, evict=5s, acquire=20s, keepAlive=true | **DO NOT LOOSEN**; already Supabase-optimized |
| **Model ownership** | sample* (tracking), shared item/order/user/merch/grid (synced/referenced) | Coordinate schema changes through `shared-db` AGENTS.md |
| **Lead-time caches** | Startup pre-builds mg_code maps from LicensingTime/FactoryTime | Stateless; no migration risk; caches refresh on boot |
| **Sample status SM** | Deterministic (direction, current status, office, event_type) → next status | Custom code; mirror in frontend `pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status.ts`; must stay in sync |
| **DigitalOcean Spaces** | S3-compatible presigned URL + public ACL | No changes needed; same bucket in sandbox + prod (`dflowbucket`) |
| **Shared database** | Consolidating DAM, CRM, PM, PLM into one Supabase project | Track `shared-db/AGENTS.md` for cross-service coordination; four-app merge protocol |

---

**Files examined:** 60+ source files; key: `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `index.js`, `models/db.js`, `models/db/*.js` (35), `models/sample*.model.js` (4), `helpers/sampleStatus.js`, `config/`, `routes/`, `controllers/`, `docs/`, `cloudbuild.yaml`, `package.json`
